Name: 58_study_notes_recall_studying
Representative_Docs: ['i spent years frustrated that no matter how clearly i understood concepts in lectures it felt impossible to retain them days later re reading notes was obviously useless but even active recall alone wasn t consistently effective i thought my memory was the issue or that maybe i was kind of dumb but then i stumbled onto something genuinely insightful the effort rule the effort rule essentially means your brain solidifies memories based on how difficult they are to initially recall but the issue is most people don t push far enough here s what finally made a difference force deliberate gaps in studying instead of neatly reviewing each topic multiple times intentionally let yourself forget slightly before recalling it s counterintuitive but creating mild stress by forgetting actually deepens memory when you struggle to recall again later convert notes into problem solving sessions instead of passive notes or flashcards i began converting my notes directly into challenging quizzes or skipping straight to past exams sometimes i was way underprepared for past exams but struggling through these sessions had the biggest impact on my retention stop creating perfect study materials i used to spend excessive time preparing the ideal notes and study setups which ended up being a form of procrastination now i quickly turn my notes into quizzes i personally use but there are other options or just chatgpt or i immediately test myself using minimal prep this way my study sessions are focused more on active learning and less on unnecessary Name: 59_managers_management_manager_decisions
Representative_Docs: ['i m working on a performance management product we have heard from some potential investors that it s a bad idea because ai will replace managers so no one will need our product so while i personally am not convinced an llm can replace good managers otherwise i wouldn t choose to work on a product where the main target user is managers it seems like some people are definitely thinking in that direction that said many managers are not good and possibly ai could replace shitty managers i m not really sure', 'whose going to manage the project to automate managers out of management imo the only thing we can t automate is management because it s the human ownership of a technical project ai no ai it doesn t matter the buck has to stop with someone and that s a variety of managers i do think there is truth to your statement though at least at my company we have no team level managers i run a team and i m a senior engineer the next layer of management is on the team of teams level i do like this and although a lot of project product management falls on me when it comes to planning and execution it s a lot easier when the managers just trust me on the technical details empower me with a team to get the job done and the right set of people to answer my questions and just step back and let me do my thing in my role the managers just worry about goals and how we allocate resources to go achieve them for me that s easy to deal with everything we do must be towards some goal i can get that done with the team and management makes sure that impact effort ratio remains high after all what i want for everyone on my team is to have a good impactful review period which translates into good reviews instead of fighting management i get a lot further by thinking of them as partners in the process where trust is a two way street and we are on the same team maybe that s niave but the only way to be trusted to build anything significant or lead is to have a manager agree to the spend', 'they should fire the managers show us how all managers can manage x the projects with the ai tools and we can talk']

Name: 60_reviewer_review_editor_journal
Representative_Docs: ['initially there were reviews and the paper was rejected however the editor asked us to resubmit if we can address the criticism we addressed the criticism in full and resubmitted the first reviewer was very positive about the changes and recommend the paper the second reviewer was no longer available so the editor asked another reviewer whose review is the one we suspect was written by ai based on that review the editor is still not letting us in unless we address the criticism of the third reviewer i think we will respond along the lines as you suggested thank you for your detailed response', 'first time trying to publish something submitted my paper qualitative study ipa psychology months ago after about months in with editor limbo it was rejected the decision was based on reviews from two referees one provided highly critical feedback some of it i acknowledge as valid particularly regarding a lack of clarity in how themes were developed in the analysis it has some over the top comments as well which i would normally not see in a standard ipa publication reviewer also called my paper unsound which as i understand it means it just isn t fit for publication anywhere reviewer gave a much more positive assessment called it important research and pushed for acceptance and rewrite the editor did not offer specific comments only the final rejection then i got another email with a suggestion of transferring the manuscript to a list of possible other journals in the same publishing house likely made with ai most of the suggested alternatives have a higher impact factors and lower acceptance rate than the journal i originally aimed for which may make publication more difficult i am currently considering three options a contact the editor to ask why a revise and resubmit was not offered i suspect this will be counterproductive so i probably will not do it b i am considering revising the manuscript in response to the critical reviewer s comments and proceeding with the proposed transfer to one of the alternative journals however i m concerned that the new journal may dismiss the manuscript outright upon seeing the initial negative review even if i make substantial improvements at the same time i would like to see this paper published as soon as possible and the transfer option could potentially expedite the process unless the presence of that first review ends up working against me c revise the manuscript and submit it to a new journal from another publisher the problem is that my topic fits only a limited number of journals and i would have to reformat the whole thing also considerably cut down on the content as the other journal i had in mind has a much stricter limit on size what should i do i got some serious whiplash reading the two reviews one was like my god you suck the other was pretty enthusiastic in tone is it common for the editor to be very conservative on the choice this is not a major journal by any means', 'i just want to share this with you all my paper on the theoretical framework for human ai communication that i developed is finally accepted and in press when i first submitted it i chose a q nature journal it wasn t specialized for my discipline communication but it was under the same umbrella of social sciences and i thought it would be a good venue plus i had no idea what i was doing it was my first time submitting a paper for publication and the apc was sky high to my surprise it was accepted for review reviewer liked it and recommended minor but significant revisions reviewer recommended major revisions i revised diligently second round reviewer was happy and recommended it for publication while reviewer still had a few minor comments which i addressed then came the third round reviewer was okay reviewer was okay and suddenly the editors brought in a third reviewer reviewer s comments were completely out of context asking for changes that either contradicted or had already been addressed per the earlier reviews it was frustrating i wrote to the editors explaining why reviewer s comments didn t align with the previous rounds and asked them to consider the recommendations of reviewers and the reply because you refused to address reviewer s comments your paper is denied for publication i was heartbroken all the exhausting revisions felt wasted eventually i moved on i submitted the paper to a top tier journal in human machine communication i found in the scopus catalog but months passed with no updates i submitted it to lower tier and emerging journals rejection after rejection by then i had gotten used to rejection i even published two unrelated papers in scopus indexed journals during that period but i still held onto hope for this framework paper then out of nowhere over a year later i received an email from the top tier journal my paper had been reviewed and they were recommending major revisions with a day deadline when i saw the comments i was overwhelmed doubt and imposter syndrome hit hard i was in a tough place finances were bad i still didn t have tenure and my wife had just given birth with our newborn hospitalized shortly after i ignored the email thirty days later the editor emailed again checking in on me saying he noticed i hadn t replied and asking if i needed more time i was touched by his kindness so i explained my situation honestly that i was overwhelmed and struggling he replied with encouragement praised the concept of my paper and told me that in their journal s culture if the reviewers see potential they ll work with you to get it published he gave me another days i was so moved by his message that i finished the revisions in less than days another year passed without updates then one random night as i was about to sleep i checked my email my paper was finally accepted the theoretical framework i had conceptualized for years was validated i wasn t crazy some takeaways it can be better to target a specialized top tier journal that understands your niche even if the wait is long rejection isn t the end of the world it s part of the process sometimes a kind editor makes all the difference if you re in the middle of this process feeling stuck doubting your expertise or tired of endless revisions keep going your work matters even if it takes years for others to see it']

Name: 64_homework_exams_grade_exam
Representative_Docs: ['students have been cheating on homework for long since ai in math the students have to do the homework honestly or they will just fail this semester for a senior level course i switched to grading homework for completion so that students that are trying to do the homework honestly don t get penalized for not cheating homework was worth and i added attendance i also added for quizzes this semester to serve as an early warning if they are not doing the work properly exams were the remaining with these policies in place the class went better than it has in the past many years some students undoubtedly still cheated on the homework but then they generally failed the exams and ended up with at best a c if they used ai somewhat for help with the homework and actually learned the material well enough to do well on the exams i guess i m just ok with that i didn t bother at all with trying to determine who was using ai for the homework the exams sorted that out but i also wouldn t go so far as to get rid of homework or make it optional as that seems like a policy that would only harm the students who were going to put the necessary effort in to the homework', 'long reply here i also teach in stem and right now only teach fully online classes with the ability to have a proctored final exam battling ai completely is impossible as we all know so we have started to adapt in how we structure the entire course not just individual problems or assessments the entire course is set up to prepare them to show they have learned the material on that proctored final exam and we tell them that from day one and we continually remind them of that each week they have an interactive reading assignment followed by a set of homework problems which we call practice because that s what it is practice for the quiz and final those problems can be regenerated with new random numbers as many times as they want with no penalty it s a small part of the grade and we have forums where they can post as many questions as they have when they re done with the practice they take a low stakes quiz review assignment which covers a bunch of important ideas with multiple choice questions and then a random sampling of five problems that are extremely similar to those they will see on the quiz students overwhelmingly say these are very helpful in a survey we give them in week they then move onto to the quiz it usually has or problems they must upload all work or it s a it s not proctored so at least one question on the quiz is graded by me in detail and they cannot get full credit for it unless they use the methods and notation of the course anti ai elements are often in place for that one special problem for example in the case of our statistics course they typically have to upload output from the software we use each output is unique because the numbers in the problems are randomized but i have code that gives me the answers to each question for easy grading also each of those manually graded problems is scored on a modified emrn scale that is common in spec grading systems only quiz scores over meet standards and help grow their grade and the one problem that i grade manually and which often has anti ai elements is worth so if they get no credit on that their quiz score doesn t meet standards if they do not get at least on a quiz they can spend one of a limited number of tokens we give them to re attempt the quiz but only after they do quiz corrections and reflections the new quiz has all new randomized problems with randomized numbers we are on a quarter system so there are roughly weeks to work which means we have quizzes we then give them a final exam review assignment because the final exam has questions one for each week of content the final exam review assignment has three randomized problems from each week for a total of problems the final exam then takes one problem from each group of three to get those exam questions again numbers are new and slight variations might show up compared to the quiz or review problems the final exam is in person they are allowed one page of notes our syllabus says that if they get anything below the highest grade they can earn in the course is a c again each problem is scored on that modified emrn scale we see several students who can t do squat on the final because they have been cheating all quarter and when we look at those low scores in detail it seems to match closely with those we presume have been taking short cuts all quarter again their right to fail all this has been a lot of work to set up but now that it s mostly done the process becomes mostly giving quality feedback on those quizzes using text expander helps greatly with all of this i can grade most quizzes in under three minutes but it takes more if they need quality feedback to help them learn from errors however i continue to add to my database of feedback and refine my teacher solutions to make that process faster when it comes down to it my job is now mainly to give quality feedback and to monitor progress continuously those who take learning seriously will tend to just fine in this system and so we focus on them now those cheating their way through quizzes are given the freedom to fail at most i might say something like i noticed your approach on this problem is very similar to what ai systems produce and leave it at that mostly an i see you comment no point deductions unless they use methods processes or notation that are clearly not in our reading materials overall this experimental approach for one course i taught as a mathematics graduate student in the us who had to manage an undergrad course i needed to do almost everything including writing the syllabus teaching the classes setting homework and exam questions grading exams and assigning grades the only thing not done by me was grading homework which was done by undergrad graders my approach worked well at least according to the student evaluation forms a bit of background on myself first i came from hong kong i did a levels in the uk and my undergrad at university of cambridge cambridge is probably the biggest proponent of the of your grades come from exams approach with exams in may june that test everything you ve learned throughout the entire year and that exam solely determines your overall grade this approach is not due to ai cambridge has always been doing that since way before ai became a thing both hk and uk tend to be more harder exams with lower grade boundaries compared to the us cambridge mathematics third year final exam is the extreme of that with crazily positively skewed score distribution something like of the theoretical full mark will usually be enough to get the highest grade which is allocated to the top of the students and the top scorer typically get of the full mark which is probably close to what a human being can practically obtain i doubt whether the professors themselves can do much better than that back to my teaching of that one course in the us i wanted to make my students grades more heavily dependent on exams due to various reasons mostly academic integrity reasons like you mentioned but at the same time i know that american students are not used to an exam heavy approach so this is what i did in these syllabus i wrote the grade comes from exams multiple mid terms and a final homework and in class work where students solve problems in class but they can talk to each other and ask me for help however homework and in class work are mostly graded on completion instead of correctness that means anyone who handed in something will get full marks in homework and in class work even if their answers are wrong as long as they put effort in it like if i can see why a student might reasonably get it wrong or being stuck at that particular step so homework and in class work effectively become attendance grade what this effectively does is that homework and in class work grades effectively become attendance grade which everyone hopefully should get full mark in and i m very lenient on homework deadline extensions and reasons or missing classes so you need to be actually extremely lazy to not get full mark in those since everyone should get full marks in all the non exam components their grade actually solely depends on exams due to these extra free points the effective grade boundary for exams is lower which means that i need to set harder exams to make it fair fair in the sense that their final grade accurately reflect their level of understanding in course materials given my background it s quite easy for me to set such exam questions and in my opinion for a course where conceptual understanding is more important than calculation accuracy which includes this particular course harder exams is needed to distinguish how well the students understand those concepts anyway an easy exam where students can get away with memorizing calculation methods without understanding them can only test how careful they are in not making mistakes not how well they understand things the only final issue to sort out is that american students are not used the hard exam low grade boundary approach they may cry and think that they re stupid if they see a in a test even if that s supposed to be a good grade and will get them an a at the end to counter that i intentionally made it so that the exams never have full mark being or numbers that can easily be mentally converted into a scale then in every exam i give a grade for this exam which comes from estimating what grade they will end up with if they perform similarly in the remaining exams in homework and exams i put stars next to questions to indicate how difficult they are for example stars mean that it s supposed to be mentally challenging and it s fine if you can t complete it unless you re aiming for an a or a at the end all the grades my students ended up with based on their scores were reasonable students who got a or a were exactly those i think having a deep understanding of concepts during in class work sessions i keep walking around and chat with students so i have a rough idea students who got a b range grade corresponded to those i think have some partial understanding and being okay in calculations students who got a c range grade are those who seem to not understand anything but managed to memorize some calculation procedures and unreliably reproduce them none of the final grades surprised me the grade distribution looked reasonable as well by standards of my department student evaluation forms seemed to indicate that students enjoyed the course not a single negative comment was there unlike some other courses i taught lol they loved the star system of indicating question difficultly the hard exam low effective grade boundary approach made them more relaxed in the sense that they can worry less about careless mistakes and more on having a good grasp of concepts given my teaching award that year i suppose the faculty was also happy with what i did']

aren t alone job seekers say the rejection comes too fast it has to be automated i m qualified so what am i doing wrong employers insist i ve used every major ats none of them auto reject resumes not even at the biggest companies so what gives let s unpack what s actually happening why your resume might never be seen how to fix it and what you can do to get more interviews with less frustration this is compiled from years of personal experience in hr tech with quotes from recruiters and hiring managers you got knocked out before anyone saw your resume most companies use knockout questions during the application process one wrong answer and you don t meet their qualifications common questions do you need sponsorship yes rejection what s your location too far rejection expected salary too high rejection some ats pre fill various questions for you after parsing your resume as a time saver make sure all of these details are correct if you are applying to a job in dallas but your location says fort worth you may unnecessarily be sent the rejection pile why some companies only review applicants within miles of the office for in person or hybrid roles your resume is missing keywords key experience and skills you might be qualified but your resume has to prove it recruiters use keyword searches to find top applicants if your resume doesn t include the exact words they are looking for you might not show up at all we have a few seconds to scan each resume if we don t see the right keywords we move on they are looking to see if you have done the job before and how great you did the job don t make them guess here s where it can be tricky sometimes even overqualified candidates get filtered out because their resume didn t spell things out hr doesn t necessarily understand all the intricacies of every role they are responsible for that s not their job for instance they might take requirements from the person or team leading tech or finance and create job postings out of that this creates a black and white scenario without much room for gray area examples you ve used microsoft azure ad but they ask for entraid it s the same thing rebranded but unless you list entraid hr might think you aren t qualified my wife a dnp fnp doctor of nursing practice family medicine once got rejected from a family nurse practitioner role for being underqualified why the recruiter didn t recognize the acronym for her degree once she spelled out that she was a family nurse practitioner she landed the next role each job has unique qualifications you need to tailor your resume and application for each one if you have the qualifications spell it out your content isn t selling your potential this isn t about keyword stuffing yes your resume needs to include the right keywords but they need to be in the proper context demonstrate you understand what they are looking for which are the key hard skills soft skills experience education and keywords they use throughout then show how you used that skill or showed that experience in an impressive and natural way strong bullets should answer what did you do how did you do it tools methods why did it matter results impact add names of programs software and systems was anything measured in values time saved performance gained or number of people impacted if you are a student you can highlight coursework volunteer experience or projects where you demonstrated what the employer wants you applied too late okay so you answered all knockout questions perfectly used all the right keywords your value is exceptionally demonstrated and you still didn t get an interview why if you applied to a job opening you expect to be considered right unfortunately that s not how ats work if you applied after too many people are already being considered you and your qualifications might not be noticed the ats sorts people based on the order they applied and if you are applicant but the hiring team found enough qualified people by applicant your application might not even be reviewed most hiring teams still manually review all of the applications this could be hundreds or thousands because this is such a manual time consuming process they start with the first applicants and only go as far as they need to in order to put a qualified shortlist together you might be the most qualified person in the entire pool and you won t even be seen if you applied too late wild right as other recruiters put it the default setting is to store applicants in order from the time someone applies so that recruiters can search and review most don t have smart filtering it s coming but it s not here tips apply as early as possible by setting alerts on linkedin google and company career sites on linkedin search jobs by past hours and tweak the url by changing tpr r to tpr r to show only jobs posted in the last hour your resume content will get you to an interview but being one of the early applicants will give you the chance of being considered it was a ghost job i hate this one but not every job posting means there s a real opening two common reasons they already have an internal candidate but have to post the job publicly even recruiters hate this my company does this all the time everyone goes through the motions knowing full well that john will be getting the job it wastes everyone s time and we all hate it but we have to do it they re not hiring yet especially after layoffs companies stockpile applicants for future openings they ll sit on qualified people to stay in the pipeline in case they get the green light to hire when they do they need to be able to bring people in quickly before their competitors do and due to the reasons above it s too time constraining and labor intensive to start from the beginning so they might make candidates wait in the process your resume format is hurting you when a recruiter scans your presentation needs to follow a simple pattern they are used to so they can easily locate key details to understand your qualifications anything that prevents this process from happening efficiently may leave you in the no pile some ats parse scan and store your resume details in part to auto fill some of the fields for you if you have poor formatting that prevents this process from happening correctly your details might be lost or in the very least populated incorrectly two or three column resumes are notorious for being difficult to scan correctly which can prevent key details from being found in relevant searches within the ats tips stick to single column layouts use plain fonts and standard headings no tables charts icons or design flourishes unless you re in a creative field a good resume should read like cliff s notes clear basic easy to read and retain the flashier your resume the worse your chances you re using ai the wrong way asking chatgpt to write my resume is a mistake why you ll sound like other applicants doing the same thing you risk fabricated details you lose your voice use ai to give you ideas as a jumping off point for writing research a company or role refine and shorten your language ai should polish your story not replace it if your writing shows the tell tale signs of ai recruiters might not trust that who you are on paper is who will show up to the interview if there is any doubt they may move forward with other candidates the ats isn t as smart as you think despite rumors most ats aren t powered by ai nor is ai auto rejecting candidates in reality ats are closer to filing cabinets with basic search capabilities many ats score resumes the scoring is terrible and most of the recruiters i know ignore them but it is a thing i ve used most of the big ones workday salesforce icims greenhouse jazz paycom beamery adp and they are generally speaking all pretty bad so we manually review applicants here are some basic facts features like knockout questions and keyword filters are set by humans recruiters search these systems browse candidates in the order they applied and humans make hiring decisions we are bound by compliance and are audited that no hiring decisions are being made by anything but a person i look at every single resume that applies to every one of my jobs and disposition every candidate swiftly if we re moving forward or not i work directly for a fortune company that has business units bottom line if ats were smarter all applicants would be seen and considered instead we are left with job seekers having to compete in a crowded broken system let s put it all together to dramatically increase your chances of getting hired tailor your resume to include all relevant keywords and experience demonstrate how you have the hard and soft skills they request use a simple resume template with outstanding content that is easy to skim apply early knowledge is power i m hoping this helps you navigate your job search more successfully any questions clarification or pushback i ve been corrected before and will happily edit this with good input']

Name: 71_generative ai_generative_genai_use generative
Representative_Docs: ['life is good for only two things discovering mathematics and teaching mathematics sim on denis poisson when you use generative ai to do something you are skipping that task if i use generative ai for writing questions or a definition then i m not discovering or teaching mathematics if one of my students uses generative ai to answer my questions then that student isn t discovering mathematics for these reasons i am utterly opposed to generative ai in mathematics education', 'hello everyone i m interested in engaging in a global dialogue about the integration of generative ai in education i ll start by sharing some examples from japan where i live keio university case at keio university in japan there was an interesting case that caught attention the pdf of the course materials included a prompt written with transparency this was cleverly set up so that if students used generative ai to write their reports the ai would generate themes explicitly unrelated to the class content university of tokyo standard at the university of tokyo widely regarded as japan s top university a de facto rule has emerged it s fine to use generative ai for reports but if you do you must clearly state the prompt i find this really fascinating with the rise of generative ai the traditional link between a student s understanding and their output has started to break down by requiring students to disclose their prompts educators can get a window into the cognitive models students are using to produce their reports to me this reflects a smart balance holding firm to the core of education grasping students comprehension and offering tailored guidance while riding the unstoppable wave of generative ai what s happening where you are i d love to hear about the initiatives being taken in your countries or regions let s exchange thoughts on how generative ai is being used in education including how perspectives might differ across cultures and borders', 'they assume that if we don t allow students to use genai in their coursework that they will be unable to use it in their jobs and will be at a competitive disadvantage to people who can like graduating a cohort than can t use a calculator and can only calculate by hand or can t do word processing and can only write by hand the analogy is bogus of course students were still being taught how to understand mathematics and how to write when calculators and word processors were introduced as tools to enable faster but correctly reasoned calculations and faster but correctly reasoned writing the majority of administrators who embrace genai and use the left behind argument to shame faculty into allowing students to use genai to complete graded assignments know full well that students are using genai as a substitute for learning reasoning writing critical thinking and course specific concepts not as a tool to help them do the brute force part of assessment faster assuming they re sincere in their belief that genai is merely a calculator like tool enthusiastic admins confuse using genai to do your homework with how writing and computing software allowed us to avoid re doing things we already knew how to do but didn t have a quick batch way of doing all at once like the thousands of simple calculations that need to be done when finding the inverse of a large matrix or auto spellchecking a report instead of having to read every line of text over with a dictionary in hand we still taught spelling after the advent of spellcheckers and we still taught how to take the inverse of a matrix before letting students loose with mathematical software packages this is because critical thinking about anything is about being able to reason from first principles genai is pretty good thought not perfect and way too people pleasing as a check work tool on a particular line of reasoning it is a disaster in its ability to simulate reasoning about basic topics from first principles however in that it threatens to substitute for a person s student s voter s etc basic reasoning skills leaving them atrophied and unavailable when needed and genai is no longer available is not reasoning correctly due to lack fo nuance and context or because human knowledge is itself biased or is no longer trustworthy due to the injection of bias i have colleagues who claim students will be left behind if not taught to use ai and i agree when it comes to the good uses of ai that do not subvert the development of reasoning and critical thinking skills and building foundational knowledge in a particular discipline but my enthusiastic colleagues aren t working to protect the development of these skills they just don t want to make plagiarism reports and get down evaluated by students by giving zeros for rampant cheating and get complaints to their boss because you can t prove it was ai etc etc etc']

Name: 72_integrity_student_academic_academic integrity
Representative_Docs: ['i appreciate your opinions on how you would deal with this in reality not theory problem my chair asked me not to report them as it will be a permanent mark on their record i like this question because my chair can be a little wishy washy so i am often in a situation like this with other things not necessarily academic dishonesty so first i try to figure out why my chair doesn t want me to report the student i say something to the effect of ok but doesn t academic dishonesty deserve a permanent mark on their record i would think the convo with the chair would go one of ways administration is hesitant to deal with this sort of dishonesty because ai policies are causing us a headache i present my chair with my clear policy of no ai use and my evidence to show that the student used ai ideally the chair says ok your case is different i ve got your back worst case scenario i realize my ai policies are weak and i need to re write them and as for dealing with the present students i guess i would say something like i m going to let it slide because my policies were obviously not clear enough i d adjust activities for the rest of the class to make them far more difficult to use with ai and focus on preventing this sort of thing in the future i just think you should be more lenient with the students i respond with something to the effect of respectfully i am very lenient with students with all kinds of issues like deadline extensions exam make ups and so on i have a zero tolerance policy towards academic dishonesty especially when i m basically a pushover on everything else my question is will you have my back while i pursue the academic dishonesty sanctions outside of those two rationales i don t even know why my chair would be resistant to reporting this kind of issue it doesn t even matter anyway the chair s job is to support you not to manage you i d hear the chair out but ultimately tell them i m looking for their support backing as chair not for their perspective opinion as educator', 'hello all i m posting here to get the professor perspective on something my wife has been dealing with this semester she is enrolled in a nurse practitioner graduate program currently i m an adjunct professor with a doctorate degree in a different health science program in our area she is over a year into her program and has earned excellent Name: 74_tutor_tutoring_ai tutor_tutors
Representative_Docs: ['i went to perplexity and i wrote i want your help to give advice to an acquaintance the acquaintance wrote i work at a community center i would like to develop a program to teach ai but i need to learn ai myself if i were to hire an ai expert what would be the cost what ai course should i start with however i would like to suggest that the acquaintance could ask you for advice about how they could learn ai and then develop a program to teach ai at a community center what should i say to the acquaintance and then perplexity responded the acquaintance can absolutely get advice from an ai assistant about learning ai and launching an ai teaching program at a community center which would help them make informed budget friendly decisions while building their expertise here s what you could say direct advice to the acquaintance it s a great idea to reach out to a knowledgeable ai assistant like perplexity for personalized advice on learning ai and creating a community focused teaching program this kind of resource is free adaptive and can guide you step by step through understanding core ai concepts with beginner friendly free courses such as elements of ai google ai essentials ai for everyone andrew ng and microsoft s ai for beginners planning educational workshops tailored to diverse backgrounds making ai approachable for all community members designing teaching strategies including how to safely introduce generative ai ethical ai use practical exercises and transparent learning materials key points to include hiring an ai expert is expensive entry level specialists cost per hour while top consultants may charge per hour or per year self learning with high quality beginner courses is cost effective most recommended courses are free and require no technical background ai assistants can help with everything from choosing which course to start with explaining concepts and drafting outlines for your own teaching program as skills progress consider partnering with local ai professionals for occasional guest lectures or project guidance instead of full time hiring sample message to the acquaintance before spending a lot to hire an ai expert you could connect with an advanced ai assistant like perplexity to get tailored advice they can recommend the best beginner courses many free help you design lessons for your community center and answer any technical questions all at no cost once you ve built a solid foundation you ll be better equipped to decide if or when you need specialist help for your program this approach is budget friendly and lets you learn at your own pace making it perfect for community education if the acquaintance wants they can start by asking the ai assistant questions like what is the best ai course for beginners or how do i structure an introductory ai workshop for adults for actionable step by step guidance', 'many academic support services like writing centers or math and stats help centers are well intentioned but often miss the mark they tend to focus on short term goals like finishing a paper or solving a specific problem rather than helping students build a real understanding of the material when students go in for help they usually leave with the assignment closer to being done but with the underlying confusion still in place this kind of support can feel productive in the moment but it risks reinforcing a pattern where students get through tasks without ever mastering the concepts behind them a big part of the issue is how these services are framed they are often promoted as the main solution to academic struggles when in reality they should be part of a larger system of support students are encouraged to rely on tutoring to stay afloat instead of being given the tools to build a solid foundation from the start the deeper issue is structural many programs lack a coherent learning ladder courses are not always well sequenced and prerequisites do not always prepare students for what comes next without that structure it is no surprise that students end up needing help after the fact what would help more is thoughtful course design that respects how learning actually happens gradually with time to revisit and apply concepts strong prerequisites and well matched corequisites give students the chance to develop skills at a reasonable pace when ideas are introduced practiced and then built upon in later courses students are far more likely to retain them in that kind of environment tutoring becomes a supplement not a crutch it is a place to ask questions and refine understanding not a last resort to keep from falling behind this brings up the role of paid tutoring which is increasingly hard to justify in today s world with free resources like youtube online forums and tools like chatgpt students have access to clear step by step explanations whenever they need them paid tutoring can feel like a shortcut but it can also create unnecessary dependence and widen equity gaps students with more money can pay for extra instruction while others are left without at the same time paid help often mirrors what free tools already offer sometimes less effectively since it encourages passive learning rather than active exploration ultimately the goal should be to help students become more independent learners that means designing programs that teach core skills properly the first time encouraging reflection and practice and pointing students to free resources that support their growth tutoring still has a role but it should not be the foundation of someone s education if we get the structure right the need for last minute fixes fades and students can build knowledge that actually lasts', 'in my experience students who seek tutoring are usually a few a students who are making sure they nail the concept this group will probably make good use of ai in speeding up learning where they can or go to human tutors at that point it will be a matter of personal preference and availability many barely passing student these students are often honest enough to attend tutoring instead of resorting to cheating attempts to pass the issue often lies in not knowing the right questions to ask since they are so far behind of course ai could in theory help but at that point you are asking a group of people who are unsure where to start to basically be a prompt engineer a decent tutor can ask a few questions at varying levels and discern from that where a decent jumping off point might be now in theory i think ai can do this but right now most models are refined to maintain the direct objective given in a prompt and follow a quite let me ask the address the next most obvious question pattern which isn t exactly how tutoring always goes the advantage of a human tutor is that they have probably encountered similar errors to the student and are able to discern to erroneous train of thought due to being there before think of a really smart math teacher who leaves you behind vs a really good math teacher who addresses your minor mistakes through problem sets step by step the thing is i don t see anyone taking the time to train an ai model on well here is what i used to think that was wrong and derailed my understanding however in the case of this group if a student figures out a way to ask a question that helps them greatly i am truly happy for them now what about the majority of average students who are smart enough to pass but complacent enough to settle for average that type of student will do the questions they find easy and call it a day when a student like that starts to fail they might resort to tutoring and be included in the group above but it is more likely they will attempt to cheat they will not attempt to cheat because of ai but they will definitely have an easier time doing so by using ai they just want enough points to pass with minimal effort there is another group of student similar to the one above the desperate grade inflation a student this student will do enough for the highest grade not put of interest in the subject or a competitive spirit but just due to the fact that anything lower than an a is their version of failure in a high performing school there will be slightly more of these and they are conceptually the same they are often just as likely to cheat but might do so more sneakily and proponents of ai use but will do their due diligence to not be caught there are students at varying grades who have an honest desire to learn and will make sure that they revisit concepts and get it right to the best of their ability ai can potentially help this group but the majority of students are just doing whatever they can to maximize their time and get the minimum grade they find acceptable this does not need to involve actually learning for many of them']

Name: 75_feedback_grading_ai grading_grade
Representative_Docs: ['the only time i use ai for feedback is to try and standardize my grades on ap lit essays i always read the student essay and assign a grade myself first then i run the same essay through two different ais to see how they grade it i have different conversation threads for each essay type and i train it by having it grade released sample student essays based on the rubric then i share college boards responses indicating the grades they gave and ask the ai to analyze what it got right and wrong and to try and give feedback based on college boards parameters it matches the college board exactly about of the time about of the time it s off by a single point of the time two or more after that i upload the prompt of the essay i m using in class and then run the student grades through it ai agrees with my initial grade or differs by one point about of the time at that point i ll look at its reasoning compared to mine and sometimes debate the point with it sometimes it convinces me to up my score sometimes i disregard it and keep my score i never lower the student grade if ai scores it lower than my own score two different ais also only match each other about of the time these are the cases where i r scrutinize myself the most after all that is said and done i submit the feedback i never copy and paste it s all the original comments i left before using ai modified using its reasoning only if i increased their score based on it convincing me and even then i paraphrase the feedback in my own words if any student is reading this wondering why it is ok for me to use it and not you you ll note that my use of it is significantly more time consuming than just reading and scoring it myself and leaving it at that my use of it is to be as objective as possible on a subjective grade and create more work for myself in trying to ensure fairness to you', 'i ve even told my students that they can either get a quick grade with little feedback in days a grade with extensive and constructive feedback i days or they can wait up to two weeks because reading their essays and constructing the kind of feedback ai can help with takes about to minutes per student and so a class of will take about hours that i have to spread out most students like and appreciate the deeper feedback this depends on what ages you re teaching and definitely at some point you have to let students make decisions even when they are wrong but you should at least be aware that there s a pretty definite answer to this question the most value comes from low latency actionable feedback even if it s less accurate or less detailed that s not to say there isn t also some benefit in delayed and more detailed feedback but if you have to choose one or the other it is the early feedback that actually helps what students prefer though is a different question from what works high latency detailed feedback definitely feels better to read not only is it generally more reliable and less likely to be off base which can be upsetting but it also arrives too late to actually do anything about it and it s much easier to tell yourself you understood and will do better next time than to actually have to go apply that feedback to your draft of this assignment and work through the details where you actually get practice and learn', 'i won t use ai for grading but i will use it for feedback you attach the prompt the rubric any other relevant information and give it a template guide for feedback and it can be an excellent partner especially when you tell it the grade tone given based on the rubric the key is to be ethical and transparent i always tell my students when ai has assisted me in feedback i won t use it for grading because i don t think it s ethical i ve even told my students that they can either get a quick grade with little feedback in days a grade with extensive and constructive feedback i days or they can wait up to two weeks because reading their essays and constructing the kind of feedback ai can help with takes about to minutes per student and so a class of will take about hours that i have to spread out most students like and appreciate the deeper feedback i read each once change it when necessary and make sure i agree with what it s saying']

subscription to its google ai pro plan after this free trial it ll revert to month here s the announcement google s bringing the best ai tools to college students for free google is essentially advertising this as a way for students to outsource their thinking while avoiding reading and writing from their own marketing materials here here homework help exam prep analyze entire textbooks up to pages why bother reading the textbook when you free get gemini summaries of its content instead writing help generate a first draft work on your arguments and fine tune your ideas who needs to write a first draft gemini can write for you polish rough drafts and refine your writing with a writing coach you don t need to write a first draft either gemini will take the first draft it generated and polish and refine it the claim about it being a writing coach is laughable writing coaches don t write for you gemini does the post discusses gemini s new guided learning mode that acts as a learning companion guiding you with questions and step by step support instead of just giving you the answer tell me google why would any student use this when they can use regular gemini and get the answer immediately under the guise of helping students learn and train in ai google hastening the demise of education to further their own bottom line google s hope is to get college students hooked on this one year free trial as a way to essentially cheat their way through a year of college and then start forking over mo when the trial ends to keep cheating now more than ever we need people who know how to think for themselves these tech companies have already done so much damage by getting a generation of students addicted to phone notifications and social media that we know is reducing attention spans and reading stamina not satisfied with the damage that caused they re now going to drive a stake through the heart of education and destroy the ability of students to think critically whatsoever sorry this is so negative but i don t want to be part of the last generation of people who knows how to think this post is also just me yelling into the void though these tech bros are going to push forward and get their money regardless of the impacts on education and society all while smiling and telling us with forked tongues that they re actually helping disclaimer none of this post what written by ai because fuck you and your insincere ai for education bullshit google', 'written by chatgpt some facts might be wrong be it could be a good laugh those times we waded through noodle diagrams and black boxes first deadly sin igor pro it s like taking off your pants to fart yet bragging about being handy if there s a top annoyances list in the research community igor pro almost always appears it claims to be user friendly with a graphical workflow yet whenever you try anything mildly advanced you re forced to write scripts in a language that feels like it time traveled from dos days an awkward mix of old school ui and scripting fire up igor pro and you re greeted by vintage looking dialogs then the minute you need a more complex function you must jump into its archaic script interface you dig through the official documentation only to find scattered fragments that seldom form a coherent method it s like rummaging in a dusty garage hoping to build a modern race car if it were just complicated you could grit your teeth and endure but it also tries to act like it s advanced yet simple which only adds insult to injury when you realize how clunky it actually is a workflow that feels like unnecessary hoop jumping in python or r you might write a few lines of code and get a polished plot in igor you open a gui fiddle with a million menu options switch to script mode edit cryptic commands hop back to the gui tweak formatting and might still end up with random export glitches it s like it wants you to use both mouse clicks and script editing in all the worst ways simultaneously leaving you exhausted eventually you start asking why did i pay for this license when a free open source library would spare me half the headache the double burden of high costs and legacy projects igor s license costs an arm and a leg meanwhile some labs have old data or custom scripts locked into igor so junior researchers are stuck continuing this tradition once you taste truly modern open source tools you realize just how liberating it is not having to dance around igor s quirks sure igor once did real work in certain specialized fields but in an era demanding code based reproducibility and open ecosystems it now feels cumbersome and antiquated like taking off your pants when you could simply do the job another way second deadly sin ni labview dataflow programming or just a tangled mess of wires national instruments ni loves to market labview as the revolutionary dataflow programming paradigm many labs jump in thinking it s a magical solution until you end up spending more time cleaning up spaghetti diagrams than doing science dataflow vs actual programming paradigms labview touts dataflow as if it s some cosmic shift in how humans code in reality mainstream computer science has well established paradigms like procedural object oriented functional or logic programming dataflow can be useful in niche scenarios but labview acts like it s the be all and end all replacing text based code with boxes and wires might seem intuitive for small projects but once things scale up loops branches submodules it s a nest of cables that would make a circuit board blush debugging or refactoring this visual labyrinth is far from straightforward noodle diagrams and hardware lock in at first dragging little boxes around is kind of fun but the complexity grows exponentially soon your block diagram looks like a bowl of tangled noodles and you can t recall which wire does what without panning all over the screen version control merging changes good luck then there s ni s hardware ecosystem pricey daq boards endless driver updates and potential licensing fees the simplest microcontroller with an off the shelf adc dac could do the job for a fraction of the cost yet ni sells you a professional solution for thousands of dollars if your setup fails or your os updates you might spend hours sifting through ancient forum posts or paying for specialized support a gimmick sold as a grand revolution the core problem is that labview markets dataflow programming like it s a brand new frontier in reality it s mostly a user friendly fa ade for basic input output control just in a block diagram style meanwhile you re locked into ni s hardware libraries and licensing once you re in it s tough to leave without redoing the entire system in a more conventional language for many the painful lesson is all this wire dragging ended up being more trouble than just writing a few lines of code third deadly sin microsoft word the pit of formatting doom a guaranteed hair puller for academic writing word is perfectly fine for simple documents but when it comes to theses journal articles collaborative editing references basically the stuff academics do daily it can become a genuine battlefield formulas figures headers everything s ready to ambush you academic writing involves formulas figures references footnotes cross links word was never truly designed to gracefully handle all these at once one inserted formula can blow up your line spacing move a figure and watch it leap to another section turn on track changes and references might spontaneously renumber you quickly learn to save multiple backups in case a single click triggers a chain reaction that wrecks your carefully formatted document collaboration reference management double trouble many labs rely on track changes so the supervisor can comment plus a citation manager like endnote the result a single word file with references and tracked changes can freeze your pc or crash outright everyone tiptoes around it hoping the next update fields command doesn t blow up the entire references list manual formatting with no reliable reproducibility word is essentially manual formatting galore if you restructure your chapters or reorder sections you re stuck re adjusting spacing headings figure layouts again there s no script or version control that elegantly separates content from layout small wonder so many scientists prefer latex for serious writing once the style is set content changes don t break the formatting with word you re locked in a constant war with your own document fourth deadly sin endnote reference management or sanity management endnote was once the go to reference tool especially back when choices were limited now that it s been integrated with web of science and stuffed with online sync features it somehow got even clunkier account sync and subscription shenanigans these days you often need a web of science account to sync libraries one glitch in the server or a mismatch in permissions and your references vanish or duplicate themselves you just wanted to have the same library on your office pc and laptop but now you have new untitled references in one and half a library missing in the other a format editor that makes you sweat adjusting citation formats should be routine you open a style editor pick your punctuation endnote s approach a cryptic labyrinth where a stray comma can throw the entire reference list off you re basically forced into a mini cat and mouse chase many give up and stick to the default endnote style only to discover their target journal wants subtle differences in punctuation or abbreviation cue another session in the endnote editing dungeon double whammy with word citing in word with endnote might feel nifty at first until your document grows references multiply and one update citations command can scramble your entire numbering or crash word then you scramble to restore a previous version crossing your fingers that it hasn t corrupted the fields meanwhile alternative tools like zotero mendeley or a simple bibtex workflow in latex might run smoother with less overhead and more transparency endnote can feel like it s adding extra friction right when you need seamless citation management fifth deadly sin spss origin sigmaplot idl ncl excel etc the old school black box brigade re inventing the wheel now we have the big motley crew of scientific software each born in a time when programming skills weren t as common some handle stats spss others do plotting origin sigmaplot some do remote sensing or astronomy idl ncl and then there s excel the universal everyone has it everyone sometimes regrets using it tool each brought convenience in the old days but also spawned a tidal wave of frustrations spss click driven statistics with hidden machinery for those who can t code spss was a lifesaver just click here for regression click there for anova but if you need more advanced or custom processing you ll find yourself wrangling with cryptic menus and unpredictable scripts debugging good luck it s not nearly as open or flexible as a real programming language origin sigmaplot the performers of visual graphs they offer specialized guis for making decent looking plots but you typically rely on a thousand mouse clicks to configure your chart with minimal support for automation or reproducibility the second time you need that exact chart you might be stuck repeating all those clicks your patience will run thin idl ncl old workhorses of astronomy weather now past their prime once upon a time idl was a cornerstone in astronomy and remote sensing ncl used to be big in atmospheric science but both are closed off or outdated sporting arcane syntax and waning community support even the original developers are nudging people toward python for modern tasks excel familiar but treacherous excel is widely available which ironically means it s often misused for large scale scientific analysis auto converting gene symbols to dates or messing with floating point data these are classic nightmares sure it s fine for basic tables and sums but for anything more advanced you re dancing with hidden pitfalls collectively these tools once solved real problems when coding resources were scarce but with modern open source ecosystems python r julia paying hefty fees and dealing with closed off mouse heavy interfaces feels more and more like a step backwards final word acknowledging their legacy yet embracing an open future we can t deny these five categories of software and their affiliates each contributed significantly in their prime labview helped many engineers quickly set up control and measurement workflows spss origin gave non programmers early ways to run analyses and make plots word brought word processing to the masses endnote introduced the concept of electronic reference management at scale idl ncl once powered large scale data analysis in science igor pro had a user base that tapped into its somewhat simpler visuals compared to raw code yet today transparency reproducibility collaboration and scalability are paramount in research tools that remain closed source pricey or reliant on rigid guis simply can t keep up with the evolving demands the unstoppable rise of open source languages python r julia proves that researchers value clarity and control over hidden processes and vendor lock in in the end nothing beats a script you can read a community you can trust and a license that doesn t cost your entire grant sometimes stepping away from these old pillars of software can feel like leaving behind old friends but if we re serious about modern reproducible science it s time to embrace truly open flexible tools and wave a less than fond goodbye to those five deadly sins end', 'firebase studio initial impressions and questions hey everyone so i ve been poking around with this new thing called firebase studio and i wanted to see if anyone else has had a chance to try it out and what their initial impressions are it seems like a pretty significant development in the firebase ecosystem aiming to streamline the whole app development process right from the browser for those who might have missed it firebase studio is described as an agentic cloud based development environment the buzzwords are definitely flying around with talk of rapidly prototyping building and shipping full stack ai infused apps it looks like it s built on the back of project idx and integrates gemini in firebase for ai assistance what i ve gathered so far from what i can tell from the documentation here are some of the key things firebase studio brings to the table in browser ide it seems to offer a full development environment accessible directly in your web browser this could be a game changer for accessibility and quick iteration ai power with gemini a big part of the offering is the integration of gemini in firebase this includes app prototyping agent prototyper apparently you can use natural language prompts including text images and drawings to generate entire full stack web applications this sounds pretty wild it s currently focused on next js ai coding assistance gemini can help with writing code and documentation fixing bugs writing and running unit tests and managing dependencies it s supposed to be workspace aware offering code completion generation and inline suggestions project import you can bring your existing projects into firebase studio by importing local archives or connecting to repositories on github gitlab or bitbucket this is crucial for adoption as starting from scratch all the time wouldn t be practical templates and samples they seem to have a good selection of built in templates and sample apps covering various languages and frameworks like go java net node js python flask next js react angular vue js android and flutter you can even create and share your own custom templates customisable environment for those who like to tinker firebase studio is built on the code oss project and runs on a google cloud vm allowing for customisation using nix this includes system packages language tooling and ide configurations you can even share your entire development environment configuration with custom templates firebase and google cloud integration it s designed to work seamlessly with other firebase and google cloud services for example you can use the firebase local emulator suite directly within firebase studio to test things like authentication cloud functions firestore storage app hosting and hosting built in tools it comes with built in runtime services and tools for emulation testing and debugging with in browser previews for web and android apps things i m wondering about and what i d love to hear your thoughts on while the feature set sounds promising firebase studio is currently in preview so i m sure there are some rough edges here are some of the questions swirling in my head performance and stability being a cloud based ide in preview how stable and performant is it in real world usage are there noticeable lags or unexpected issues effectiveness of the ai the ai assistance sounds great on paper but how effective is it for more complex coding tasks and debugging does it genuinely speed up development or is it more of a novelty has anyone tried the app prototyping agent for more intricate applications integration with existing workflows while importing projects is good how well does it integrate with existing local development workflows if you need to switch back and forth customisation depth the mention of nix customisation is interesting but how accessible and well documented is this for developers who might not be familiar with nix pricing and workspace limits currently there s a limit of three free workspaces per user with options to increase this by joining the google developer program or subscribing to a premium plan what are the long term cost implications likely to be especially for teams also it mentions that using certain integrations like app hosting might require a cloud billing account and could upgrade your firebase project to the blaze plan and your gemini api usage to a paid tier it would be good to hear if anyone has run into unexpected costs data privacy the documentation mentions how your prompts responses and code might be used for model training and how to opt out what are people s thoughts on these data usage policies use cases what kind of projects do you think firebase studio would be best suited for is it more for rapid prototyping or could it become a primary development environment for larger applications comparison to local development what are the main advantages and disadvantages you see compared to a traditional local development setup with a local ide easy to understand the documentation has a feedback section asking if the content was easy to understand based on your experience how clear and comprehensive is the information available for firebase studio overall thoughts so far on the surface firebase studio looks like it could be a really interesting tool especially for those heavily invested in the firebase ecosystem the ai integration has the potential to significantly speed up development and the in browser environment could lower the barrier to entry for some however being in preview there are definitely questions around stability performance and long term viability so has anyone else had a chance to play around with firebase studio what are your initial impressions and what are your biggest hopes or concerns let s discuss looking forward to hearing your experiences and insights']

Name: 78_proctoring_exam_lockdown_browser
Representative_Docs: ['hello professors and academic leaders i m a computer science enthusiast developing a capstone project that addresses a concern many of you share cheating during online exams especially with the rise of ai tools like chatgpt and other web based resources that are hard to control during remote assessments the problem online proctoring has focused largely on webcam surveillance and browser lockdowns but students are still managing to copy paste code use ai assistants or switch tabs undetected while simultaneously raising valid concerns about privacy violations our proposal we re building a lightweight privacy respecting app that runs only during online exams and is designed to detect intentional dishonest behavior here s how our minimum viable product mvp works key features monitors the student s screen captures a few screenshots per second not full video just enough to detect irregular behavior track their iris movement through their web camera and to maintain the privacy only the vector logs will be sent to the server no facial record will be stored or collected flags suspicious activity like copy pasting answers from other sources typing ai related terms e g chatgpt or gemini switching away from the exam window repeatedly running virtual environments or developer tools built in tamper detection if the app is disabled or if students try to run it inside a virtual machine it automatically flags this behavior ethical safeguards no facial detection or audio access nothing personal is getting stored no full keylogging only flag based logging no long term data storage students explicitly consent to all monitoring how it works educator summary students take the test using our app which locks focus to the exam environment and monitors only the screen and keyboard activity related to cheating teachers will assign login and password to our app to the students and will monitor them through web the final product will be able to support thousand candidates and will be scalable if a suspicious action is detected it raises a red flag with supporting evidence e g the text that was copied or keywords typed only the websites allowed by the teachers will be accessible privacy first we ve taken careful steps to ensure the tool remains non invasive and compliant with academic and ethical standards students are informed and must agree to terms before starting no hidden monitoring only minimal information is collected no access to personal files photos camera or audio source code will be partially open for transparency why we re sharing this we want input from real educators would you find this approach useful in reducing cheating while protecting student trust are there any ethical lines we re close to crossing even with consent and transparency would your institution consider piloting or advising a solution like this a call for feedback do you think this tool could be a legitimate alternative to intrusive proctoring would you as an educator feel comfortable recommending this to your department what improvements would help make this more aligned with academic values thank you your feedback means the world to me i m doing this as part of my academic journey but also because i believe there s a middle ground between over surveillance and open book chaos if we get it right we can help universities conduct fair assessments without sacrificing student dignity or privacy warm regards', 'as a ta and program admin at a top university i ve spent a lot of time researching and interviewing the major players in the proctoring space honorlock examity respondus and others what i ve found is that the solutions schools are paying for are not only ineffective but also a huge waste of money here s the problem current proctoring tools are riddled with gaps that students exploit and they often create more headaches for professors than they solve here s a breakdown of why current solutions fall short inability to monitor beyond the browser most proctoring tools like lockdown browsers or browser extensions only monitor activity within the browser this leaves everything else other apps desktops or external devices completely unchecked students can easily alt tab to note taking apps messaging platforms like discord or even google docs if they have multiple monitors those often go unnoticed too lack of system wide monitoring many solutions can t monitor processes running on the operating system this means students can open documents run unauthorized apps like chatgpt or virtual machines or even use remote desktop software all while the proctoring tool remains oblivious insufficient detection of external devices lockdown browsers and extensions can t detect secondary devices students often use smartphones tablets or even a second computer to cheat privacy and ethical concerns both professors and students have raised concerns about how invasive proctoring tools can be there s a lack of transparent minimally invasive solutions that balance academic integrity with privacy cheating through virtual machines students can bypass lockdown browsers by running exams in a virtual machine vm while doing other activities on the host system limited multi monitor support many tools can t effectively monitor multiple monitors or even confirm if a student has unplugged additional screens difficulty scaling for large classes current tools are often hard to scale for large classes especially when manual intervention like live proctoring is required over reliance on live proctors many solutions require live proctors which drives up costs and creates logistical headaches especially for exams in different time zones it s also invasive and awkward for students false positives and administrative overhead professors are often overwhelmed by false positives eye movements lighting changes or random sounds flagged as suspicious complicated exam setup many proctoring solutions have onboarding processes that take months that s ridiculous it should take minutes to set up an exam no solution for admissions interviews during admissions interviews cheating is rampant scripted answers searching for answers mid interview and more yet there s no tool to address this after getting frustrated that none of the proctoring tools were effective and or really fit a modern day way of teaching plus just unreasonably priced i assembled a small team and created this let me know if more of you have had the same issues i m happy to provide access to the tool for free to try out', 'as a
