Name: 128_study_flashcards_studying_notes
Representative_Docs: ['studying can be a pain especially when you have hours of lectures and pages of notes to get through i started using study snail and honestly i wish i had found it sooner turn pdfs into study sets just upload a file and it auto generates flashcards quizzes and a chatbot to break things down no more manually making study materials summarize youtube lectures instantly paste a video link and it pulls out key points generates a study guide and lets you ask questions about the content perfect for last minute review ai chatbot for studying it doesn t just give random answers it actually helps you learn by generating questions and breaking down concepts i ve been using it for a while and it s honestly been a game changer anyone else using ai to study would love to hear how you re making it work', 'i ve been diving into ai powered study tools lately testing different platforms to see which actually help with studying one tool that stood out was study snail and here s why study sets upload a pdf or paste a youtube url and it auto generates flashcards quizzes and a chatbot to answer questions it s a game changer for reviewing lecture slides videogpt paste a youtube link and it summarizes key points generates questions and lets you chat with the video perfect for condensing long lectures ai chatbot for studying unlike chatgpt it s designed for studying so it focuses on creating actual study materials instead of just giving generic answers of course i also tried other tools like insert ai tool name and insert another ai tool name each had its strengths but study snail felt the most seamless for turning content into actual study materials curious if anyone else has tried ai for studying what s worked for you', 'hey everyone getting motivated by my own struggles i made an app called scholaroos an ai powered study companion that helps you learn smarter not longer here s what it can do ai study chatbot chat directly with your uploaded lectures and notes ask questions simplify difficult concepts take quizzes or dive deep into topics all powered by scholaroos intelligent chatbot with intelligent modes ai summaries upload text or audio lectures notes or even scientific papers get instant well organized summaries that save hours of review time ai flashcards automatically generate high quality flashcards from any content and review them using the built in spaced repetition algorithm sm for long term retention visual learning add your own images or handwritten doodles to summaries and flashcards to strengthen memory organized workspace manage lectures in folders bookmark key materials and tag with custom labels for quick retrieval multilingual support scholaroos now supports languages like spanish hindi arabic chinese and more so you can study and chat in your preferred language i built it because i used to spend hours re reading notes before exams now i just upload my lectures and the app helps me study more effectively and stay consistent it s free to try and i d love feedback from students who actually study with it if it helps even one of you ace your next exam it ll be worth it apple app store google play store']

Name: 129_swes_swe_password_replace swes
Representative_Docs: ['speculation on why ai is sold as being able to replace specifically software engineers anyone who s ever had a product they were trying to sell has exaggerated how great it is imo that s what s happening here if ai could legitimately replace swes then it would be a huge win for companies that employ swes because swes are expensive hence there is an incentive for ai companies to suggest that maybe it s possible either now or in the near future', 'i don t think anyone truly expects ai to fully replace swes end to end but it will allow swe to do the work of swes and pretty soon this same process quartered the automotive industry in america about years ago', 'it s already happening you just don t realize it replacing swe jobs doesn t necessarily mean swes are no longer needed just that fewer of them are needed to do the same amount of work as before ai isn t to the point where it can entirely a swes job but any developer who has invested in learning how to use it as a tool will tell you that it absolutely does increase productivity increased productivity means fewer devs needed per product so yes to some degree we have been replaced and ai will continue to increase productivity which will reduce the need for swes on a per product basis resulting in further replacement whether or not this increase in productivity will result in a total reduction of swe positions is entirely dependent on the demand for those software products if demand increase at the same rate as productivity the market will remain constant']

Name: 130_gpu_decentralized_streaming_sync
Representative_Docs: ['i studied physics and later worked at my university i m sure many of you have experienced the same need compute for ai simulations but every time i spin something up i end up facing the same issues your job is in queue alright guess i ll check back in hours spot instance disappears mid run love that for me bill arrives why am i being charged for a gpu i never used and then there s the gpu problem do i really need an h or will an a do the job and how do i find the cheapest option that still gives me the performance i need i m currently working on a product that aims to simplify this whole process for scientists and experts in their fields who cant be bothered to manage their own infrastructure no more cluster battles no begging admins no more confusing aws pricing and always the right and most cost effective gpu for what you actually need i am building a demo and would love some help any chance you could share the problems you re facing i d love to know where it hurts so i can make a cool product', 'hello i m working on a vehicle detection application using yolov integrated into a fastapi web app the system uses vlc rtsp url and streams the camera feed in real time i ve been running the application on a cpu no gpu and i m using the yolov s model specifically optimized for mobile use in hopes of balancing performance and accuracy my setup backend fastapi vehicle detection yolov s using the mobile version of yolo camera feed rtsp url streamed via vlc hardware running the application on cpu no gpu acceleration model loading load yolov model once globally device torch device cpu model torch hub load ultralytics yolov yolov s device device the challenges camera feed lag despite adjusting camera parameters frame rate resolution the video feed lags considerably making it difficult to stream smoothly detection inaccuracy the lag significantly impacts vehicle detection accuracy as a result the model struggles to detect vehicles properly in real time which is a major issue for the app s functionality steps i ve taken tried using various yolo models both the regular and mobile versions but performance issues persist experimented with camera resolution and frame rate to minimize lag but this hasn t resolved the issue optimized the loading of the yolo model by loading it globally once yet the lag continues to affect the system system limitations since i m using a cpu i know that yolo can be quite resource heavy and i m running into challenges with real time detection due to the hardware limitations i m aware that yolo can perform much better with gpu acceleration but since i m restricted to cpu for now i need to find ways to optimize the process to work more efficiently questions optimization how can i improve the performance of vehicle detection without gpu acceleration are there any optimization techniques specific to yolo that can be leveraged for cpu based systems real time streaming any suggestions for Name: 131_coworkers_clients_saved company_information
Representative_Docs: ['continue life you have another problem the data that your coworkers enter into chatgpt is often added to the training set this means that you can leak your clients proprietary information if you put it into chatgpt and you are correct ai is not intelligent it is a useful tool sometimes but it hallucinates answers and it always sounds like it knows what it is talking about often being confidently dead wrong ai right now is nothing but really really advanced auto complete it doesn t think it is not conscious it is a statistical technique congratulations on the promotion you did not torpedo your coworkers they spoke out of ignorance and it was recognized by the bosses you may have saved the company this is the kind of crap that loses clients', 'dude i use ai all day and i have to say you did the right thing ai is great if you need basic text that will not severely alter someone s business life like say you had a small company and needed some bullshit filler text about how much you re company cares about it s clients chat gpt is fantastic for that need a touch of css to make your front end look pretty it s not super good at that job but it can spit something out that s mostly functional but advice to clients about niche legal and financial information is not what chat gpt is good at in fact it s down right dangerous to assume it can do anything that specific and sensitive you probably saved your company from a huge liability by stopping them you objectively did the right thing by both the company and the clients your coworkers if they re decent people will be able to recognize that they made a mistake and they need to work to fix it i m not sure if you can really do anything to make it up to the coworkers who decide to stay mad at you when they were the ones who fucked up some people just want someone to blame who isn t themselves you can t change that tough spot to be in but that s the reality i m sorry if they give you trouble you type of small company that are my most frequent clients i knew that some of the senior employees used it but i honestly didn t know they would take offense to what i said i swear one of my older coworkers laughed a bit and said that i should stop being paranoid and cited a case where she talked to a client that wanted an specific information about accounting she s a specialist in marketing and she only managed to give him the information while using chatgpt i guess i was a bit offended because i wouldn t usually do it but i immediately said that i understood her point but that the information she gave the client was absolutely wrong this sparked a small back and forth because another coworker said i was silly for wanting to know more than the machine until it was solved by my supervisor actually looking up the real law of our country that confirmed i was right we sort of laughed it off afterwards and i didn t think much about it but yesterday my supervisor came to talk to me because our boss wants me to take on a bit more responsability for a while because some of the senior coworkers were going to take obligatory training essentially our boss went to investigate further and it was revealed that an over reliance on ai tecnology has led to wrong information being given to dozens of clients he also asked me to make a document with essentials to know about accounting to appropriately address the demands of companies i have a degree in accounting they are apparently also going to have to take an ethics class because of the silly and paranoid comments my Name: 134_quizzes_questions_online_quiz
Representative_Docs: ['i ve been toying around with questions that would undermine ai google use i ask students to limit their responses to course material only multiple choice questions might include correct answers that utilize material that we aren t covering the class essay questions would be marked wrong or down significantly for using concepts ideas definitions material that isn t in the course material i might even ask questions that are unrelated to the course material in a multiple choice question the correct answer would be this was not covered in the course material simply adding that option to all multiple choice questions would force students not to rely solely on google but actually gasp look at the course content i might give options for a short essay question choose one of the two prompts to write a response on if the prompt is asking about material that is not covered in the course material do not select that prompt to write on so it would effectively be a meta quiz where they need to select the proper prompt to answer answering the wrong prompt would earn a zero would this be unfair', 'where s small success sunday i ve got one for that anyhow just finished grading a new assessment task that i ve run all semester students have to do readings and post discussion questions based on these to the discussion board before class then in class they discuss in groups based on their posted discussion questions with one student each week reporting back to the whole group only three groups report back each week and which three groups is a closely guarded secret so no one knows who is going to have to stand up and talk so better be prepared mwaahahaha how do i get them to actually post discussion questions i hear you cry assessment backwash of course my dear beleaguered colleagues they are graded on a quality of their discussion questions b participation in said discussions c evidence of critical thinking and d quality of their report back to the whole group everyone gets one go per semester i m pretty gentle on the presentation and participation in discussions it s really hard to get anything below an a but i m really interested in their questions and evidence of critical thinking those i mark with more rigour i was blown away by the quality i had not expected to get so much clear evidence of their doing the readings as i progressed through the list i could see the patterns a top marking students had devoured the readings and were asking questions i would like to chew over myself i was giddy with excitement with these ones had to stop myself gushing in the comments b a decent tranche of students who had clearly done most of the readings and were coming up with some solid discussion questions that even if they weren t ground breaking they were sure to keep the group s attention c a smaller tranche who hadn t done much of the reading but at least were interested in the course content enough to ask some genuine non ai questions d only a few students tried to skate by with ai crap there were probably some in b and c that had maybe learned some better prompt skills but i don t think i missed too many i did give a couple the benefit of the doubt aside from the delving and consider this then that part of what gave them away were funky html tags you can go into canvas discussion posts edit their post and then swap to html view to see them no student is writing div data start tags i think this might be from google notebook lm but boy oh boy i m pretty happy either way the level of engagement it was bloody hard work marking all the moving parts but it is definitely one i will run again with some tweaks', 'i ve taught large classes too and the writing is a hard issue to deal with i ve used multiple choice and while it s easy and good questions can be written takes time and care it is also not a very good learning tool it s mostly passive assessment if you are using canvas and have a ta there are some really interesting things you could do i have found that small quizzes linked to specific concepts and as preparation for larger tests is a pretty useful model you can use mc fill in the blank and maybe even a short answer or paragraph essay type thing which your ta would correct the small quizzes are low stakes and they can all be self correcting the exams are then longer and are higher stakes i have found that mixing short and long high and low stakes benefits students you can also include not just straight questions but a video that they have to analyze or a clip of you lecturing on a topic that they have to respond to or a small passage from a reading they have to write a response to this last one isn t quite the same as a reading test you give them the passage but they have to know context and the overall concepts to be able to answer at all well so you provide an incentive to read and provide some synthetic learning and get an assessment at the same time your ta can correct those another method with canvas is discussion questions these are labor intensive but the ta can grade them you set a question the students have to respond to the question and also respond to one or two other students answers students actually tend to like this the hard part is grading the discussion responses but that s where the ta comes in and you have to provide a minimum word count or sentence count for responses to students or the students or some of them will just say that s great you could even grade them just giving credit based on meeting word count or your ta could grade content or both there s also a tool in canvas where you can set quiz questions inside a video either multiple choice or fill in the blank i think that they have to answer while watching it s been a while so i can t remember what the tool is or how to use it but a little clicking around or asking an it support person could probably solve that anyway it s a good learning and assessment tool if this is an online class you don t say you re stuck with the limitations of canvas but if it s a face to face class using canvas one thing you can do is have certain assessments online and then have essay exams in person using bluebooks or whatever no ai issues then and the ta grades the written exams there are a lot of creative things you can do to cut down on your work but actually increase student engagement']

Name: 137_abstract_paper_read_shoe
Representative_Docs: ['i am going to keep this vague since my goal is to not name and shame with this post i am one of a few people reading through abstracts for an upcoming conference long story short we got to a paper about ai digital humanities and ethics on the surface it seemed interesting but the more we read of this particular abstract the more off it seemed it used extremely stereotypical language as well as several of what i would call buzzwords that felt from disconnected from eachother and from the paper s topic in addition the author stated that they would use literary examples in their presentation but then didn t actually say what the examples were after our meeting i couldn t shake the feeling that something was off so i decided to input the abstract into the various softwares that predict if something was written by ai on every tool i used it came back as written by ai i have a feeling that this person didn t have a paper ready and they threw some ideas into whatever ai they used with the goal of writing the paper later i am not one of the people that feels as though ai is evil but there has to be a line drawn somewhere it s one thing to use ai to write an abstract for you which is still lazy and terrible but for a proposal on the social and moral implications of ai to be written by ai simultaneously hilarious and depressing', 'not for nothing but in addition to the irony of you relying on an ai detector to detect the ai that you re complaining about which others have pointed out and i get that you re dismissing it but putting that aside this actually sounds completely reasonable if the paper is arguing pro ai you were vague and that s fine but from what you ve given us i have to assume that the paper is arguing in favor of accepting ai as ethical because if they used ai to write an anti ai paper that would be beyond dumb and given your stance i would assume you d have mentioned it and if that is indeed their stance then i would expect them to use ai to assist in the writing of the paper because your stance that there has to be a line drawn somewhere it s one thing to use ai to write an abstract for you which is still lazy and terrible but for a proposal on the social and moral implications of ai to be written by ai seems to be presuming the outcome that the paper would be arguing against as a fact rather than allowing them to make the argument which makes me wonder why you d be soliciting for papers debating this if you ve already prejudged what the argument should be that feels disingenuous again i don t know this paper it might absolutely suck i have no way of speaking to the merits but it seems as though you re asking us to object to the general principle of them engaging in the practice that you have asked them to argue in favor of and that feels anti academic in a way', 'i d like to share something i ve been noticing as a reflection on what seems to be happening in a number of academic adjacent online spaces there is a massive uptick in the amount of ai theories being posted in various academic subreddits and it s becoming both concerning and a nuisance i frequent r academicpsychology and i ve noticed it is particularly bad there see this original post for the most part i will be restating what i said in my original post because i believe it applies to the broader scope of academia creating a post with ai is about as low effort as you could do mind you this is coming from someone who has no issue with ai when used as a learning and efficiency tool the issue here has nothing to do with formatting and everything to do with the degree of effort invested in articulating original ideas the half baked material people present with these ai theories which are far from even a well thought out notion read like pure nonsense i don t care if you like how your ideas sound what undermines credibility is outsourcing the entirety of the intellectual labor to ai read an actual paper at the very least demonstrate that you care enough about your own ideas to actually express them yourself if you could not have arrived at this grand framework without ai s assistance then perhaps the idea is not yours to claim and if it truly is your work then you should be able to present it independently without relying on an automated system to do so this is not what it means to use ai as a tool how can you say i used ai as a tool when the tool in question ai is creating your thoughts or at least feeding them back to you in such a manner that you snowball into some grand epiphany loop i have no issues with ai as a tool this is not what a tool does even if the nonsense you re spewing is true if what you re saying is so monumental then articulate it in your own words in such a way that we feel it is deserving of our time to read look at it like this if you bought a bunch of fancy ingredients wagyu steak caviar truffles etc and told me you were going to make me a michelin star meal but then you blended it up and poured it into a cardboard cup and served it to me of course i m going to focus on the means and not the meaning i don t care what s in the fishy meat smoothie i wanted my michelin star meal the same applies here whether or not you ve concocted something actually meaningful doesn t matter your message gets lost in the way you presented it Name: 141_fact check_evaluate_rome_infographic
Representative_Docs: ['the first thing is they need to have some level of knowledge about how to fact check so if chatgpt is describing a study for instance they need to know something about that type of study and how to interpret it so i don t think students who are very early in their academic journey are equipped to evaluate ai output until we give them the skills to critically evaluate certain forms of evidence one can examine the sources cited are those sources real and do those sources actually support how they are cited by ai so the student would try to track down cited sources do they even exist and once the sources the ones that exist are obtained the student could systematically check the claims from ai against the actual source i used to have students do this with media stories about research findings compare the journal article report of the study to the media report and see what s missing or inaccurate that was eye opening for some students i imagine doing the same with ai could be useful the downside is that sometimes ai is accurate so a student could track down a source and find out that ai was accurate in its description the student may then conclude that ai is always right i know that s not a smart conclusion but that s how some people think', 'courtesy of my online class they were instances where they used ai and failed spectacularly first not very epic but the class was tasked with do something rather simple chose a pre columbian tribe from the territory we now call latin america and do an infographic with info such as religious cosmological beliefs hierarchy geographical location etc most did a good job i mean you can t really go wrong with that unless you literally copy and past info i had a student turning in an ai generated infographic and as you know ai seems to be at war when generating text in an image you couldn t read a thing and the images were really wonky plus the usual people who just copy and paste text on an image and call it an infographic the second one is much more epic and concerning and i gave them a good chewing because it was more than one person doing this they had a task of pointing out on a map of the world certain locations that were key locations during the medieval era to give an example ireland rome and syria for the life of me i don t know what prompt they gave to the ai or what program did they use but most put rome in the atlantic ocean syria in either the caspian sea or england and ireland in morocco the best and most concerning part was that they actually pin pointed each location like that excuse me rome in the atlantic ocean did i miss something or are they not teaching basic geography in schools anymore also whatever happened to critical thinking at least double check the information for god s sake ai can be a fun and very helpful tool if used right i personally use it to organise times on my lessons since is something that has gotten a bit more difficult for me for some reason i guess is because i have been assigned lessons as of late i know daunting so i use a lot of activities to keep them sharp and give breaks and it has worked great when dividing my time between the topics that i have to cover in class', 'tips for professors using ai in coursework assignments clarify expectations up front set boundaries be explicit about when and how students can use ai tools e g brainstorming editing coding assistance not for final answers require disclosure ask students to note how they used ai in their work like a sources consulted section align with learning goals make sure students understand that ai is a support not a substitute for their own reasoning use ai for assignment design brainstorm prompts use ai to draft different types of assignment questions and refine them for rigor generate examples quickly produce practice problems worked out examples or alternative phrasings of the same concept vary difficulty ask ai to produce tiered questions basic advanced to scaffold learning encourage productive use by students drafting aid students can use ai to overcome writer s block or generate outlines then revise critically coding helper for programming courses ai can debug or explain code but students should annotate their use language support especially useful for esl students ai can help polish grammar while they focus on ideas build critical thinking into assignments ai comparison tasks ask students to critique or fact check an ai generated response reflection components require a short note on what ai got right wrong and how the student improved it process over product grade partly on drafts iterations or reasoning steps not just the final answer use ai for teaching workflow rubric design have ai suggest grading rubrics or criteria you can refine feedback draft feedback with ai then personalize quickly for each student admin tasks summarize readings generate quiz questions or create discussion starters avoid pitfalls hallucinations remind students ai can make things up cross check with credible sources over reliance stress that deep learning comes from grappling with material not outsourcing it equity provide access guidance so students who don t already use ai aren t disadvantaged would you like me to sketch some sample assignment structures that explicitly integrate ai e g use ai to generate then critique then improve so you can see how this plays out in practice do you want me to also include a shorter reddit comment length version like a condensed checklist in case you want something that fits better in a fast moving thread']

Representative_Docs: ['i ve hosted several educational webinars to generate leads and drive engagement and this webinar software called webinarninja has consistently delivered the best results it s access and robust engagement tools make it my preferred platform every time', 'there are a few solid webinar platforms out there depending on how much emphasis you re putting on interactivity vs ease options for follow ups livestorm a solid all in one option for webinars with great engagement features polls q a upvoting they also have ai powered insights on attendee behavior bigmarker offers a ton Name: 147_survey_articles_papers_publishing
Representative_Docs: ['i can t believe the academic dinosaurs are against a tool like ai s normally this type of survey or article doesn t specify how the ai is used during the process so the main critique comes from people thinking that there are people out there writing a whole manuscript with ai yes everyone can agree that this is unprofessional ridiculous and embarrassing but i am glad to see that this one asks better questions why on earth are people against ai drafting a section based on outline points isn t that the same as what the pis have historically done asking their phd students to do that if it s a draft that the researcher reviews corrects and supplements there are low chance of hallucinations false data or citations an important part of a researcher s job is communication and this has many forms not just knowing the language well enough to write congruent sentences in a way that the journal likes communication means using all of the tools at your disposal to in your judgment give a message in the best way this can be using ai if english is not my first language or if i you are not good at writing making an interactive presentation if your audience is young make good graphics that people outside the field can understand etc etc', 'nature s survey laid out several scenarios in which a fictional academic named dr bloggs had used ai without disclosing it such as to generate the first draft of a paper to edit their own draft to craft specific sections of the paper and to translate a paper other scenarios involved using ai to write a peer review or to provide suggestions about a manuscript dr bloggs was reviewing see supplementary information for full survey data and methodology and you can also test yourself against some of the survey questions survey participants were asked what they thought was acceptable and whether they had used ai in these situations or would be willing to they were not informed about journal policies because the intent was to reveal researchers underlying opinions the survey was anonymous the respondents were contacted in march through e mails sent to randomly chosen authors of research papers recently published worldwide and to some participants in springer nature s market research panel of authors and reviewers or through an invitation from nature s daily briefing newsletter they do not necessarily represent the views of researchers in general because of inevitable response bias however they were drawn from all around the world of those who stated a country were from the united states from india and from germany for instance and represent various career stages and fields authors in china are under represented mainly because many didn t respond to e mail invitations the survey suggests that current opinions on ai use vary among academics sometimes widely most respondents more than think it is acceptable to use generative ai to edit one s research paper or to translate it but they differ on whether the ai use needs to be disclosed and in what format for instance through a simple disclosure or by giving details about the prompts given to an ai tool archive today version for if you re paywalled', 'why publish an academic article if the answer is to introduce a little known or complex subject to a wider audience then as long as it is accurate and passes rigorous peer review it should not matter if it was the result of years study or drafted by martians the idea is to make the world a better place by getting the information out if on the other hand the reason for publishing is to tick boxes towards getting a grant renewed or a push up the pay scale then it does matter but this latter reason for publishing is silly and not in the wider academic best interest it is just an administrative convenience if the threat of ai drafting of articles makes universities employers etc come up with better way to truly gauge the abilities of students employees etc then that is a good thing those bodies would be better off working out how to more usefully gauge the abilities of their students or employees than pondering ways to stop unstopable ai being used but that does lead to another question and an example i am a historian in my s retired and no longer attached to any institution i am also fascinated by ai in practice and theory and love messing with it i have been meaning to write an article about a largely ignored early th century spanish text that throws a fascinating light on my area of study it is quite hard to understand and has a lot of maths in it so it s been on the back burner this morning i decided to try an experiment i have the text as a pdf it was printed in the s i fed this into notebook lm and got what that calls a briefing document about the text i then copied that into gemini flash and told it that it was a specialist in the relevant subject and to write an academic article based on the briefing document complete with abstract and conclusions it whirled away for seconds and then came up with a word article that i reckon is of the way there it would need some editing robust checking historical context added some footnotes etc etc but all quite an eye opener i reckon it needs just a few days work to put it into a submitable form i want the information to be out there because i believe it to be of interest to a particular group of people i don t need the brownie points for saying or implying that i did it all by myself look mum no ai but that leads to the question if ai i do publish this or other historical articles after due peer review of course how do i we fairly state that']

Name: 148_academic misconduct_misconduct_academic_professor
Representative_Docs: ['what you re describing raises some legitimate concerns about procedure every university that enforces academic misconduct procedures is required by its own internal policies if not by law to provide written notice of the allegation an opportunity to review the evidence and a fair chance to respond before any sanction is imposed from what you ve written it doesn t sound like you ever received a formal notice hearing or explanation of the process only that you were accused submitted documentation and were still found responsible if that s accurate the issue may not be the accusation itself but whether the university failed to follow its own student code of conduct or academic integrity policy even private institutions are generally expected to honor their published procedures once disciplinary action begins a false academic integrity accusation can be devastating especially now that schools are still developing policies around ai use you would have the strongest grounds for challenge if you can show the university denied you basic due process no notice no hearing and no genuine opportunity to respond before the sanctions were issued you might start by requesting in writing a complete copy of your academic integrity file and the university s policy governing these cases that documentation will tell you whether they followed the steps they were supposed to from there if the process was skipped or mishandled you could explore an appeal or speak with an education attorney about next steps', 'i m almost done with second year of my bachelor s degree one of my final assignments grades weren t uploaded and it came with a comment from the uc saying he cannot mark my assignment yet as there s some concerns on the assignment and to wait for further communication from him or an academic integrity panel i panicked since that was the last thing i expected to see in this degree journey but obviously panicking doesn t help at all guyss so stay calm if this happened to you anyways i had to let it go and see when they ll get back i went on with my life for three months and all of a sudden i got an email mentioning that i have been accused of a serious academic misconduct case and was given the option to attend a meeting with the panel according to the allegations and possible outcomes in my university s procedures unless i can prove the legitimacy of my work i will be suspended or even expelled for serious academic misconduct cases like this after attending the meeting it was clear that the reason for this accusation was my work flow was not great universities detect the editing time of your documents and they can also see copy pasted material so unless you have drafts or notes of the assignment you can t really prove that you did the work yourself it basically indicates that you ve copy pasted from ai or through someone else i provided an explanation of my workflow and how and why this document shows those paragraphs to be copy pasted material tip is to really know your workflow and explain each step of it and also keep drafts of your assignments or try to work on one doc by saving it onto your drive initially before starting your assignment so it will have the real tracks of the assignment and track of your real editing time rather than it just showing minute or minutes i used scribbr for some referencing which is not wise and i had made some errors in my references always double check references as apparently it s the most important part of your assignment most importantly i took accountability for this action and mentioned moving forward i ll work on my referencing as being transparent will help you trust me on that turn it in similarity scores really matters universities don t emphasize this enough there really is no safe turn it in score try your best to have it honestly as low as possible excluding the references anyways with much prayers and adequate proof that i presented to the panel the outcomes were disallowed marks for a couple of assignments and dismissal of the case for the other units which is not bad compared to the serious outcomes they also mentioned that the violation will be indicated until i graduate but afterwards there wont be any record of it as this is the first case', 'this incident has been so so overwhelming and frustrating basically i was given a on a draft assignment due to ai use i think the reason being that i made some citation errors that i guess are indicative of ai use i obviously went on the defense said i was more than willing to provide version history and prove i didn t use ai but was ultimately shot down because the issues would be serious violations regardless of whether i used ai or not okay but the issue here is that i was explicitly told my grade was because i used ai so i consult with several other professors and all of them appear to agree that these serious violations are generally acceptable mistakes during the drafting process we re all human this means that my version history becomes even more important because it proves these mistakes were genuinely that and not done with any malicious intent but again the professor is not interested in seeing it this professor had previously kind of insinuated i used ai when i didn t but they did not outright accuse me of anything until this assignment so i just let it go it wasn t some of my best work anyways the professor ultimately starts trying to downplay the ai accusation saying it was initially overemphasized and insisting that i was still violating the academic code of conduct despite me offering to prove that these serious violations were a not malicious and genuinely part of the drafting process which again my version history could prove and b not the work of ai they ultimately bump my grade up saying they had reflected on the kind of professor they wanted to be first year professor first time teaching classes not as a ta and first time teaching a graduate class but that they would not be coming to an agreement with me that i didn t use ai so how am i supposed to continue in a class where i m afraid to turn in my work because i m not even given the chance to prove i didn t use ai my tone and voice in all of my assignments going forward will remain the same moreover after attempting to find human common ground in something along the lines of it sounds like we re both learning and adjusting to our respective roles i was told that bumping my grade up was mercy and that we were not equals in our roles which i never said we were in conclusion i guess my biggest mistake was not disputing any insinuations i d used ai right off the bat i just kind of figured based on the language being used as well as subsequent emails to the class that it was a class wide general warning thing i m currently torn between just cutting my losses and dropping this class or sending something to the head of their department i m not sure if it d be a formal complaint or what i genuinely don t care about the grade for the assignment what makes me upset is that i wasn t heard or given the chance to properly defend myself and when i tried to find common ground in order to salvage at least some sort of collaborative beneficial relationship going forward i was belittled i hope y all can learn from my experience whether you re dealing with students turning in work that is potentially ai or accused of doing so yourself and if anyone has any advice going forward it would be much appreciated']

Name: 153_phd_advisor_supervisor_lab
Representative_Docs: ['i am an th year phd student in a top us university e g harvard mit etc i am basically done with my phd i just want to share some of my shitty experiences and just looking for any advice or opinions you may have on topics including should i continue in academia if given a chance basically my phd advisor doesn t have any idea about what research can be done previously he gets grants to collect some special data and just have his students analyze these data using conventional methods or some of his students have even relied on something they learned from an undergraduate project the latter is increasingly the case right before i started as those grants to collect data became much harder to obtain since there isn t much work left to do in that entire field of study i wasn t able to take my qualifying exam at the end of my second year due to the lack of a project there was an earlier project based on a small tweak from an undergrad project then the phd project of a former student but since neither of me and my advisor knows much about that undergrad project the very small tweak invalidated the whole idea for a simple reason unworthy of publishing and i was careful to not object my advisor too early so it took us years to finally accept that i was left to my own devices to find a project my qualifying exam was put on an indefinite hold i almost fainted while describing this situation in the office of someone in charge of graduate studies in our department it was extremely stressful several months for me at the end i basically acknowledged that it was over there was no way that i could actually propose a full fledged multi year research project however this acceptance of defeat gave me much calmness and without going into the details it actually helped me to find a new quite theoretically deep way to address a long standing controversy in the field at first everyone required proofs and careful study on what i found i delivered just that slowly but concretely i proved my discoveries with both theoretical framework and computational and real data the first paper was published in my th year years from when i didn t have anything to talk about on quals which was indefinitely delayed on one of the journals that s just below the nature subjournal in our field two more papers came later but i never reached nature or nature subjournal level it was extremely frustrating during these years working on these papers not only because neither me nor my advisor knows anything about the basics of the main topic or the many tangential areas so everything has to be dealt with slowly and carefully learning from scratch but more importantly i always felt that the whole point of the project was under question since i was the one who started all of it and there always could be some small mistake i made that could kill the whole project just like how the project in my first two years of phd was killed also i know i was on my own no one s got my back i tell everyone i know that i am intimidated from doing research this was not only a mental burden but seriously changed how i lived my life i gave up way too much i could have enjoyed be it taking an interesting class engaging more in community activity or thinking alternative career options etc all just because of the deep insecurity i have about my core task doing the research i don t suffer from this insecurity anymore as the method is established and the infrastructure on how to easily apply it are built what makes me suffer is that my advisor then gave the method to his new students one of them was able to publish a paper on the same journal as my first paper in just year now this new student is in his th year and he has two more forthcoming papers one in a nature subjournal and another one in the same journal as his and my first paper he has officially surpassed me in his quality and time greatly of publication and well on his way to a year phd all these while living stress free i am sure he has his troubles but comparatively he has suffered nothing compared with me knowing there are people who got his back if the project has problems and able to pursue many other hobbies in his life including building an alternative career option ai based in case academia doesn t pan out my advisor is currently repeating this process again with a new st year student what they are doing is basically just applying my method blindly on different datasets it s very easy to apply now and doesn t even require understanding of any of the theoretical and computational details i performed during my phd leading to the method which i know they do not understand fully an analogy is like you don t need to know how a car works to use it to get you to places also note that none of these were even in my advisor s traditional fields not the main topic nor any of its sub components this was another problem that bothered me and his th year student that since we are not well known in these fields although the science was absolutely correct the work is not getting cited much since we the student advisor are really outsiders to these research fields and this effectively stalls our career in academia hence a real need for an alternatively career plan but sadly i did not have the spare time to seriously consider it for a long time i believed my advisor s problem is just his academic incompetency but with what he is doing with his new students i am really very disgusted with his character sure he realizes the value of my discoveries now and what he is doing is just advancing science he also knows exactly what i had to go through to get every single piece of knowledge in these fields i have today i got them not to do a mundane job or for personal pleasure they were there building up a wall defending my existence as a phd student how could he hand over a project someone fought so hard to establish so easily to someone else and let these new comers to harvest the ripe fruits while leaving the original farmer who planted the seeds in order to avoid death to starve to death also if i have a chance to continue on to an academic job which is not high right now why couldn t i lead my own students to do these projects he is currently having his new students doing he was not even in this business or its tangential before i think he is hiding behind the name of advancing science but in reality he sensed an opportunity to go back to his old comfort zone where he can just use a routine method on different datasets and write up reports on that how easy is this to produce phds in batch this easy life until his retirement he has his last student now is too exciting that made him forgot about anything else including that he has now ruined Name: 155_pdf_pdfs_vision_ollama
Representative_Docs: ['hi everyone i m the founder of notalyze ai and i wanted to share something i ve been working hard on i ve spent years struggling with massive pdfs disorganized notes and time consuming reading whether it was academic papers lecture slides or technical docs i always thought why can t i just upload a pdf and take structured intelligent notes instantly so i built notalyze ai a tool that turns static pdfs into dynamic ai powered notes you can ask questions directly in the pdf extract key ideas instantly take structured notes using headings bold even code blocks all in one clean distraction free workspace it s built for students researchers and professionals who want to learn faster and retain more without the overwhelm it s currently in beta no credit card require d try it here i would love your feedback ideas bugs even brutal honesty your input means the world thanks for reading bravin founder', 'hey everyone i ve been testing a few tools for serious pdf work apps that actually help you read explore and understand your documents instead of just summarizing them these are the four that stood out most to me notebooklm askyourpdf pdf ai and pdfury i tried all of them personally and want to share just my honest impressions so you can pick what fits your workflow best notebooklm pricing free notebooklm by google focuses on summarization and concept generation rather than deep document reading pros completely free automatically creates summaries flashcards and concept maps can generate video or audio summaries good for idea visualization and overview cons doesn t display the actual pdf only extracted text difficult to verify which source each answer uses not suitable for research or detailed text analysis best for creating summaries quizzes and quick conceptual overviews askyourpdf pricing limited free tier paid plans month askyourpdf is one of the more established and feature rich options pros side by side layout pdf on the right chat on the left works with scanned pdfs ocr easily chat with some or all of your documents at a time screenshot q a ask about images or figures directly share documents via link clean functional interface chat export for saving conversations offline cons occasional lag or layout shifting shared chats aren t real time each link creates a copy to another account source highlights are sometimes inaccurate best for users who want a stable rich featured document platform pdf ai pricing limited free tier paid plans month pdf ai is simple and well designed though somewhat heavier in performance pros side by side layout pdf on the left chat on the right supports scanned pdfs ocr includes screenshot capture and a chrome extension exports chats as pdf files cons slower interface upload limit mb on paid plans source references appear only in the end and can be off topic best for small scale academic or business document work pdfury pricing limited free tier paid from week or month pdfury combines a simple intuitive design with great speed and collaboration tools pros side by side layout pdf on the left chat on the right inline source highlighting shows exactly which text was used screenshot q a ask about images or figures directly collaboration mode real time shared chat anonymous users can view in read only mode chat export save the entire conversation as a pdf for offline use works with scanned pdfs ocr fast and minimal interface cons doesn t support chatting across multiple pdfs smaller ecosystem than the older platforms best for deep reading research and collaborative studying summary feature notebooklm askyourpdf pdf ai visual pdf view scanned pdfs ocr collaboration partial real time source highlights text only end of message sometimes off end of message sometimes off inline precise chat with multiple pdfs limited chat export video audio summary flashcards quiz generation pricing free mo mo wk or mo if you just need summaries or flashcards notebooklm is excellent but if you re working with complex or academic pdfs and need to verify sources and actually see your documents then askyourpdf pdf ai or pdfury are far better options Name: 156_masters_ms_ml_ai ml
Representative_Docs: ['hello i am a final year undergrad from an iit i am part of a dual degree program which has an integrated m tech b tech over the course of years we are required to write a master s thesis as a requirement for completion many european uni s that i have come across only take in masters student for their phd i am looking to apply for different phd program related to robotics and ai targeting eth uzh and other schools do these schools consider an integrated masters a complete masters degree in this sense for context i have sufficient research experience as an undergrad with a tier first author publication and couple of co authored papers would these help justifying the integrated masters as a complete masters it would be a lot of help if someone could clarify this thanks', 'hi i am planning for my masters and i am bit confused here so i want to pursue masters in ai ml and don t have interest in core cs subjects but i see lot of people do masters in cs with ai specialization and take a job in ai or ml field but i don t understand what is the merit in doing so can someone suggest this based on their experience that ms in ai is better or ms in cs with ai related subjects', 'everyone is going to tell you something different and biased here s some perspective it comes down to what you want out of it the actual program offered by the uni and the industry where you live if you want to be creating new ai ml algorithms or pushing the boundary further effectively research most of those jobs exist in us europe if you aren t there and require phd level research experience and significant output in research it s also an intersection of computer science and maths most people here are more theoretically maths stats heavy think advanced stochastic theory measure theory etc if you want to be in the camp of majority of people here software engineers that want to be implementing building or having sufficient conceptual knowledge around ai ml then you can get it from any combination of uni self study work and practical experience most unis offering these programs are masters by coursework some might have a small thesis component or project at the end a masters by coursework is just units subjects similar to your undergraduate most people take them to shift into a new field a master by research is for those considering dipping their toes in research effectively create new body of knowledge and how to build on others research it s more likely for someone to upgrade their masters by research to a phd they are two different purposes for your situation you could consider a masters coursework and supplement it with practical experience and self study the industry moves fast and you still won t learn things that people talk about now that trendy like mcp ai agents llm rag all those tools people talk about like cursor claude etc these are just implementations practical applications and they will change quick some will be adopted and accepted and other dropped the masters program would be useful if you need a planned study program and if you wanted theoretical foundation that underpin ai provided the uni program unit such as statistical learning machine learning deep learning reinforcement learning bayesian theory how hopfield neutral networks work monte carlo etc work remember ml ai is actually intersection of cs and maths so when you pick a uni program make sure it has a balance of cs and stats if you really wanted to go deeper your maths level will need to go deeper']

Name: 157_stop using_hard work_learn_learning using

Representative_Docs: ['as someone who works in ai the problem you are facing is the result of using an ai that has been trained generically to do something specific if you learn how to build and train models to be more like you it will produce better results critically the reason you should be encouraged to build a model more suit for your purposes is students are increasingly getting their education from generic models being used and without differentiating they will become generic themselves', 'the solution is so simple don t use ai to do the work for you invest time effort determination persistence and hard work to actually learn like people did some years ago before ai in its current form was available but deep down we all know that we aren t using our minds as much as we used to at least in my case sorry but you only speak for yourself and that is a you problem your lack of effort and outsourcing to ai caused that', 'stop using ai and start investing effort to actually learn there is no secret sauce it s all hard work how do you think people have learnt before ai years ago or even before the internet they didn t lazy out like you did they worked hard you just went to the gym to watch the spotter do the lifting thinking you s gain muscle that way go cold turkey on ai']

Name: 161_accounting_accountants_excel_accountant
Representative_Docs: ['accounting will be replaced with ai', 'so i ve been making this analogy for like years now and i ve had a lot of people tell me i m wrong because obviously ai is just going to keep getting better and better and take over more of what developers do in a way that excel couldn t for accountants i think there are two really important things to understand about what excel did whether you think they re analogous or not excel automated like of the time that accountants spent doing bookkeeping before excel companies would have a bunch of people whose job was to literally write down and track financial transactions by hand if you go back before computers this was all done in pen and paper like i worked with people who were old enough to have done manual bookkeeping in their lifetimes but bookkeeping was not is not never has been the value driving contribution of accounting bookkeeping was a necessary evil it was the base level of what you needed to do to make sure that you were keeping accurate track of your money where accounting has always delivered value is in taxes and identifying financial patterns trends outliers that are relevant to business operations so this is where things get intersting before excel let s say bookkeeping was like of the man hours spent in an accounting department so if excel is automating of the you would conclude that excel has now eliminated the need for like of all accountants right that would be a huge disruption and yet that is not at all what happened why because bookkeeping was of what accounting used to do not of what accounting could do and that is exactly what happened today accountants spend of their time on bookkeeping and yet the accounting profession has blown up in terms of importance because now every accountant is largely focused on activities that deliver value', 'accounting has always been an area ripe for outsourcing traditional automation and now ai tasks like bookkeeping invoice processing data entry reconciliation and sample audits using ai ml have steadily become more automated over time in many companies it departments still report to the cfo because finance and accounting were often the first areas where businesses introduced computers to automate work and as always if you can t automate it you outsource it there are literally hundreds of thousands of people in the philipines who are outsourced contractors doing accounting review for big companies most people are now familiar with the copilot model of ai you don t replace entire departments but instead more senior team members have boosted productivity and don t need juniors to do grunt work anymore even if that s where they would normally earn their chops accounting feels especially susceptible to this model even more than programming a cpa and years of experience are still valuable but the edge coming in without experience is shrinking even if ai doesn t continue to improve at its current pace and entry level accounting positions are underpaid which makes it less attractive in general rule based automation for structured data has been around for a while but recent ai advancements have made it possible to automate tasks involving unstructured data like documents for example let s say businesses communicate internally through tools like slack salesforce and zendesk as a side note there are ai and search tools gaining popularity that can index and search that data to act as a knowledge store with references links to sources you ask it questions off your internal documentation and that s an ai use case that s legitimately working pretty well but externally companies mostly communicate with each other through documents contracts pdfs invoices purchase orders order forms etc and yeah of course less structured stuff too like e mails these documents require legal review but there s also a lot of manual accounting review work involved to look at the financial implications of different contracts track revenue report on it and audit the process now ai tools that can scan documents and extract or flag specific types of information are becoming more common converting unstructured data into structured data for easier review with a high level of consistency and the ability to reference sources no one actually likes doing this review but it s pretty manual work stuff like invoices are generally not super complicated and there s still going to be some manual review work involved but it s just one area task in the accounting field where ai does have a legitimate boost to efficiency and a lot of cfos caos controllers and director of accounting folks love this stuff of course there will still be high trust high skill areas forensic accounting full audit prep and execution complex tax strategy interpreting financial data where a real accounting background remains essential and you wouldn t trust that to an ai but there s a lot of areas where ai has an opportunity to carve out work the open question remains about the quality of implementation the level of trust in the system speed cost and if even ai is worth it over just outsourcing']

Name: 163_plans_lesson_lesson plans_district
Representative_Docs: ['years asshole a time saver and force multiplier are literally the same thing i m sure you do pull the same shit out of your saved files every year which were likely passed down to you from another teacher when you entered the profession probably have a lot of word banks and factual questions too here s an ai prompt i used last year to help me build a lesson on the mexican revolution i am teaching my ap world history class about the mexican revolution and want to design a collaborative role playing game there will be six groups each representing a different faction within the mexican revolution i would like you to design introductory role sheets for each of the six factions that will require my students to individually read about two pages of background information on their faction to help prepare them for a debate i would also like you to generate a list of four pressing issues within the mexican revolution as a group the students figure out how their faction would have felt about each issue so that when they debate they can represent their side could i do that on my own sure but how many hours would it take i dunno probably it takes me about times as long as the class period to design a lesson from scratch how long did it take with ai about minutes including typing the prompt its a fucking force multiplier dude', 'i don t use ai to create my lesson plans i use my very own brain', 'okay so i ve been that teacher frantically googling ai lesson planning tools at pm on sunday nights for the past year burned out th grade teacher here ell classroom and i was literally drowning in differentiation requirements while trying to maintain some semblance of work life balance spoiler there wasn t any the problem let me paint you a picture i was spending roughly hours every weekend on lesson plans not teaching not grading just planning my district requires differentiated materials for reading levels ell accommodations and everything aligned to state standards by sunday night i d have bloodshot eyes from staring at my laptop creating multiple versions of the same worksheet my ai tool graveyard i tried everything chatgpt fast but pedagogically questionable gave me a lesson on photosynthesis that completely ignored misconceptions kids have magicschool feature overload still had to rebuild most things eduaide better but felt like it was just remixing the same templates diffit good for reading levels but thats about it various others that shall not be named because they were genuinely terrible most of these tools are what i call wikipedia summarizers they can regurgitate information but have zero understanding of how kids actually learn like none of them caught that teaching fractions before establishing number sense is a recipe for disaster enter teachshare i was very very skeptical when i found teachshare because their whole pitch is tiered instruction and i m like yeah sure everyone says that but i found this through a leslie fisher webinar so i figure it was worth giving it a shot the first thing that caught my attention it asked me about my specific classroom demographics not just grade level but how many ell students reading level distribution cultural backgrounds previous units covered already different from the one size fits all approach i d seen everywhere else the game changer here s what actually shocked me i gave it my standard test create a lesson on the water cycle for th graders with mixed reading abilities and spanish speaking ells not only did it generate differentiated materials but it included cognates for spanish speakers evaporaci n evaporation flagged common misconceptions like thinking clouds are made of water vapor suggested formative assessment checkpoints based on actual research created materials that scaffolded from my students prior knowledge i literally sat there staring at my screen because it incorporated things from marzano s research that i learned in my masters program like an ai that actually knows instructional design theory real numbers my sunday planning sessions went from hours to about minutes but here s the thing the quality is better my admin actually complimented my differentiation strategies last observation and my ell students are more engaged because the materials actually speak to them the multilingual magic this deserves its own section because holy shit it doesn t just translate it adapts culturally when i create a math word problem it adjusts contexts for different cultural backgrounds instead of johnny has apples it might use maria visits the mercado for my spanish speaking students with vocabulary that bridges both languages being honest here sometimes i still need to tweak things about of the time i have lost my design once or twice sometimes has overlapping content the verdict look i m not saying ai will replace good teaching it won t but teachshare is the first tool i ve found that seems to actually understand that teaching is both an art and a science it s like having a teaching assistant who actually studied education theory who this is for teachers drowning in differentiation requirements anyone with multilingual multicultural classrooms people who care about pedagogy not just speed teachers who want evidence based practices without reading research papers who should skip if you just need basic worksheets this might be overkill if your district has zero flexibility in lesson formats if you re looking for completely hands off still requires teacher judgment happy to answer questions or share specific examples also created a sample unit if anyone wants to see what the output actually looks like and before anyone starts pointing fingers no i m not affiliated with them just a tired teacher who finally found something that works and figured others might be in the same boat we deserve tools that actually understand education not just automation']

who worked for the people who worked in tech with few alternative options in between and no this isn t ai i just like using em dashes anyway this is all to give context to three truths our district Name: 166_illiterate_literacy_functionally illiterate_functionally
Representative_Docs: ['speech to text autocorrect or ai there are people who are functionally illiterate too about of americans are functionally illiterate which i believe is defined as those who cannot read or write above a third grade level', 'it s a multi faceted problem it s not just the whole language model it s that the culture has changed it s that technology changed how children think i feel like functionally illiterate isn t self explanatory as a term kids can read but what they struggle with is comprehension they are poor at abstract reasoning imagining perspective determining credibility deciphering purpose they can move their eyes across the page but they can t expand beyond literal meaning whole language model may impede kids from proficiency at a critical moment but kids have a much shorter attention span shockingly so now and their relationship with dopamine is different now with ai they don t have to write or research and they don t check for accuracy functional illiterate is more akin to being media illiterate but at a larger scale they don t find reading enjoyable even if they did they can t focus they don t see reading as having a point they can t see the meaning of text it s not just whole language i wish it was because we could simply go back to phonics and fix the problem for gen beta but it s not it s a hydra of a problem and it s not just an education issue it s the parents it s the culture it s the technology like people scream about functional illiterate kids but those kids still get a s our standards and priorities have changed critical reasoning and abstract thought have been taken for granted as skills all humans just naturally develop kids can just get a s for average work kids can and do cheat at a shocking level compared to ever before their entertainment and social sphere is attached to them at all times anywhere in their phones it s easy to blame the admittedly shitty while language model but the fact is the problem is much much bigger than that and it s getting worse', 'i think there s a misunderstanding of the functionally in the phrase functionally illiterate being interpreted to mean something like virtually effectively etc illiterate even op rewrote what i assume was functionally illiterate where they originally saw it as basically illiterate in their post which i assume is because of a misunderstanding of the phrase people think it s an unnecessary modifier like saying literally in front of obviously literal or obviously figurative phrases when talking about a literacy crisis i think every article i ve seen specifies that they re talking about functional literacy meaning the ability to go further than just reading the words functional literacy is being able to interpret meaning from text follow instructions fill out forms etc this can be taught and is the primary focus of language arts education once you get passed very early foundational stuff the issue is that honing this skill comes from reading outside of school when your only reading is being done on the internet through forums and comment sections you lose out on practicing these skills i don t think this is an effort by the media to discredit schools or manufacturer a crisis it s just a result of the way children have been allowed unfettered access to the internet and now ai and have been given less structure at home when it comes to supplementing the education done in school']

take ai reviews are helpful but not foolproof they re great for spotting syntax issues code smells and repetitive patterns suggesting best practices or improvements in structure reviewing large chunks of code quickly but they often miss contextual issues specific to your architecture or domain deeper design concerns or trade offs subtle bugs that require runtime understanding security or performance implications in real world environments that s why tools like codesherlock ai are gaining traction they aim to combine ai speed with real world quality checks it s still smart to follow up with human reviews for critical projects but using ai as a first pass reviewer is already a great practice bottom line use ai reviews to augment not replace your code review process especially for learning or early stage development', 'have you ever had ai do a code review we enabled github copilot reviews for all of our pr s to see how it d go almost every comment the ai reviewer makes is Name: 169_summary_reading_read_cliff notes
Representative_Docs: ['use ai as a drafting and differentiation helper not as a worksheet factory if op enjoys designing keep that use ai to scale differentiation and accommodations i build my own core tasks then ask a model for three leveled versions ell friendly directions under words five exit tickets tied to the same standard and a quick misconception list i paste my answer key so outputs align then i edit hard and verify sources i ve used magicschool ai for iep scaffolds and diffit to level readings smodin helps with quick paraphrasing and plagiarism checks on student drafts keep the human touch use ai for drafting and differentiation not as a worksheet factory', 'sure i ask questions that require them to reference details or examples from the reading but make them open ended enough that there are multiple possible correct answers so that they don t have to essentially memorize every word for example what is one example author gave to illustrate concept x explain how that example relates to x for a reading where x is a central concept and there are paragraph long examples of x what is one of the three reasons author gave to support conclusion y explain this reason in detail for a reading that argues for conclusion y as its central thesis with two page sections focusing on reasons and which example of phenomenon stuck with you the most describe it in detail for a historical or descriptive reading that includes several vivid often horrific examples that you couldn t forget even if you wanted to what is one thing you didn t understand from this reading or one thing you wanted to know more about what made it confusing or what specific questions do you have for a difficult reading what is z according to author for a term that could have many possible definitions but only one that s correct for this reading or where there is a mismatch between the technical meaning and the ordinary meaning the key thing is that ai is vague and generic ask them for things a human would remember but that require detail and specificity or holistic comprehension or niche knowledge ai is fundamentally vague generic and conventional', 'it s kind of like the lawyer joke never ask it questions you don t already know the answer to at least of the time it fucks things up in profound ways usually because it defaults to i m going to confidently spit out something that could sound good then when i point out the mistake it apologizes and we roll the dice again all that to say yes it can absolutely be an amazing tool if used wisely by professors but if your goal is to teach students to develop the skills and thinking required to read an opaque challenging text and summarize it accurately then it doesn t matter whether the ai result is accurate or not the student has bypassed the thing they were asked to do to demonstrate mastery the hard reality is that having students do something outside of class on a device is no longer reliable for evaluating their mastery or knowledge has anyone tried saying you guys are welcome to ask ai for a summary or the top points of what you ve just read but only after you ve actually read it that just seems so naive of me they will probably use ai either way if you tell them ai is okay for x under y conditions they will use ai for everything under all conditions and later claim they only used it for x under y conditions in my experience i mostly teach first years your seniors will be more savvy at covering their tracks']
